directions: Combine the vodka, lemon juice, and sugar and pour into a cocktail shaker with ice. Pour into martini glasses and garnish with lemon slices.

name: Frappuccino Coffee Cocktail
directions: Mix drink and vodka to Shaker with ice and shake to mix.  Strain into 2 cocktail glasses and enjoy!

name: Pink Greyhound Cocktail
directions: Pour all the ingredients into a tall glass.  Stir to blend.  Fill glass with ice.

name: Barefoot Contessa's Pineapple Margaritas
directions: Assemble in pitcher. Keep in fridge. Shake with ice before serving.

name: Cucumber Tequila-Citrus-Mint Fizz
directions: Cucumber tequila:  Pour tequila into pitcher (save bottle); stir in cucumber.  Refrigerate overnight.  Using fine mesh strainer and funnel, strain tequila back into bottle.  Store in refrigerator up to 2 weeks.
Cocktail directions:  Fill 12-oz. tumber with ice.  Pour tequila over ice.  Squeeze lemon and lime wedges over ice; add to glass.
Put mint leaves in one hand.  Clap between hands several times to release oils; drop leaves in glass.  Top with soda.  Stir gently.

Tip:  If you don't want to make a whole bottle of cucumber tequila, you can cut the quantity down to make just what you will use up the next day.  (i.e. use 4 - 6 ounces tequila and about 6 thin slices of cucumber.).

name: Margarita Recipe
directions: Shake with crushed ice in a cocktail shaker and pour it all in a margarita glass rimmed with salt.

similar.name: The Margarita Recipe Everyone Asks Me For!

name: Champagne Punch
directions: ****To make simple syrup, boil 2 cups of sugar and 2 cups of water in a saucepan over medium-high heat until the sugar has dissolved. Remove from heat and let cool completely before using. To store, refrigerate up to 2 months. This makes 3 cups.
In a pitcher, combine ice, fruits, lemon juice and simple syrup. Slowly pour in 1 bottle of champagne or white wine. Serve.

name: Peachy Cooler
directions: Fill glasses with ice cubes.  Add all ingredients and stir well.
If desired, garnish with lime slice.

name: Screw Me Silly
directions: Mix all ingredients and serve over ice.

name: Easy White Sangria
directions: Combine all ingredients.  Allow to marinate for at least 24 hours, preferably 48.  You can keep this in the refrigerator in a cool, dry place.
Serve over ice and enjoy!

name: Aperol Spritz
directions: Fill a champagne flute with Aperol.
Add Prosecco and top with a splash of soda water.
Garnish with a lemon or orange slice and serve.

name: Bubble Yum (Vodka Drink)
directions: Place ingredients in a blender and blend!  Yummo.

name: Ginger Caipirinha
directions: Muddle the 2 Tables of Sugar, Fresh Lime and Crushed Ice.
After this, add the slice of fresh ginger.  Muddle some more -- but do not break ginger.
Add more ice and Rum.
Shake.
Serve.

similar.name: Lemongrass, Kaffir Lime and Ginger Mojito

name: Heath Bar Coffee
directions: In a large  12 oz. mug, mix coffee, syrup and cocoa.
Add steamed or heated milk to taste and enjoy!

name: Lavender Cosmopolitan
directions: Rub the rim of a martini glass with a lime wedge and dip into lavender sugar. Combine the cranberry juice, vodka and parfait amour in a martini shaker. Shake well and pour into martini glass.

name: Chocolate Covered Cherry Martini
directions: Add all three ingredients in a Ice filled Shaker and Shake till really cold.
add to a Pre chilled Martini Glass ( To chill: Before you begin add ice and cold water to your glass) When you have finished shaking your mixture.
add to your pre-chilled glass and garnish with a cherry or you can dip your cherries in chocolate and freeze and add to your martini.
I promise you wont be disappointed.
I saw this recipe at a restaurant in New Jersey it was great.

name: Iced Cafe Reggae
directions: throw all ingredients in a blender with some ice.
blend for 30 seconds or until frothy.
enjoy.

name: Blue Cowgirl
directions: HALF-FILL A COCKTAIL SHAKER with ice and add the gin, blue curaçao, and Cointreau. Cover the shaker and shake vigorously until well chilled.
STRAIN THE COCKTAIL into a martini glass, garnish the rim of the glass with the lime slice, and serve right away.

name: The 3-Carat Ruby
directions: HALF-FILL A COCKTAIL SHAKER WITH ICE and add the lemon and lime pieces, vodka, Cointreau, and sugar. Muddle to blend the ingredients and to extract juice and other essence from the citrus fruits, covering the glass with one hand as much as you can while muddling. (It can be a messy technique, but muddling is a classic bar method that draws out more flavor than simple shaking does.).
STRAIN THE COCKTAIL MIXTURE into a highball glass filled with ice, and top with the soda and cranberry juice. Serve right away.

name: Twisted Fresca
directions: Fill a glass with ice. Add Amaretto, Triple Sec, and fill glass with Fresca. Enjoy!

name: Apple Cider Vinegar Health Tonic
directions: Mix ingredients in a glass. Drink on an empty stomach in the morning. Feel great.

name: My Dads Chai Tea
directions: Boil tea
Then add spices and simmer 5 minutes.
Add milk and heat through.

name: Sunny Apple Cider
directions: in dutch oven combine all ingredients.
cook over medium heat until heated through and flavors are blened, about 15 minutes.
Tip: To keep cider warm, hold in crockery cooker on low temperature.

similar.name: Warm Slow Cooker or Crock Pot Cider

name: Orange Berry Blossom
directions: Add all ingredients into a cocktail shaker with ice, shake and strain in an old rocks glass. Garnish with a slice of orange.

name: Oh-So-Easy Sweet Tea
directions: Fill you're 10-12 cup coffee maker's pitcher with water and pour it in the machine.
Tear open 2 family size tea bags and put the tea in a coffee filter in your coffee machine's basket.
Turn on the machine and let it do all the work!
When all the tea has brewed, pour it into a 2-quart pitcher.  Add the pinch of baking soda(to cut down on any bitterness) and the cup of sugar and stir until all the sugar is dissolved.
Cover and chill in the fridge or serve immediately in glasses filled with ice.  Make sure there's a little plate of lemon slices available for anyone who prefers lemon in their tea.
You can make this with decaf tea, but it won't taste as good.  Trust me, I've done it.
The baking soda may sound strange, but don't leave it out.  It cuts down on the bitterness of the tea, making it more smooth.

similar.name: Perfect Sweet Tea (Secret of the South)

name: Blackberry and Cabernet Caipirinha
directions: Combine blackberries and quartered limes in a large pitcher and muddle until well crushed.
Add cachaça, Cabernet, lime juice, orange juice, simple syrup, cranberry juice, and some ice and stir to chill thoroughly.
Garnish with lime and orange wheels and serve.

name: Caramel Mocha
directions: In a small dutch oven, combine sweetened condensed milk, caramel topping, and chocolate syrup.
Stirring constantly, cook over medium-low heat about 8 minutes or until mixture is well blended and hot.
Add coffee; stir until blended.
Serve hot.

name: Shirley Temple
directions: Pour ingredients over ice in a tall glass. Garnish with cherries.
Enjoy!

similar.name: Saucy Shirley  (The Good Sip Soda Pop)

name: White Chocolate Martini
directions: Place chocolate syrup in bottom of martini glass.
Use a toothpick to "drag" the chocolate syrup up the side of the glass.
Place liquer, vodka and ice in cocktail shaker.
Stir until well mixed
Strain into prepared glass.

name: Jet Ski Killer
directions: 1.  Fill a shaker with ice.
2.  Pour spiced rum, coconut rum, orange liqueur, creme de banana   liqueur and pineappple juice into shaker.
3.  Shake vigorously.
4.  Pour into a tall glass with ice.
5.  Garnish with a cherry and lemon slice. Enjoy!

name: Miami Heat Martini
directions: Combine vodka, lemon juice, sugar and grenadine in a cocktail shaker, half filled with ice cubes.
Shake well and pour into glasses.
Garnish with maraschino cherry and orangel slice.

name: Gameday Lemon Drop Jello Shots
directions: Heat the 8 oz. of water to boiling.  Disolve Jello for 2 minutes.  Add cold water and liquor, stir until combined,.
Pour into small plastic cups with lids (you can get these at the liquor store or restaurant supply company).  After you are done pouring, cover with lids and place in fridge.
When the jello is almost set, sprinkle sugar on top.  Not too much.  I just do one small pinch in each cup.

name: Jager Bomb Jello Shot
directions: Heat the whole can of Red Bull until boiling.  Dissolve orange jello for two minutes or until completely dissolved.
Slowly add water, vodka and Jagermeister, stir until combined.
Pour into little plastic cups, cover with lids and place in fridge until set.
I usually make them and have them set overnight so that the flavors can mingle together.
You can get the little plastic cups at the liquor store or a restaurant supply company.

name: Sangrita Rooster Tail
directions: Mix all the sangrita ingredients together and chill for at least 2 hours (needs to be very cold).
Serve the sangrita in a highball glasses (can garnish with a fresh chile pepper and a sprinkle of cumin).
Serve the tequila in shot glasses beside the rosy sangrita.

name: Pineapple-Cherry Amaretto Sour
directions: Mix well and serve over ice.

name: Lollipop (Drink)
directions: Fill a tall glass with ice.  Pour shots in order into the glass.
Stir drink or you can use a martini shaker to mix the ingredients, however you want to do it.
Top drink with a cherry and enjoy!

name: Brodie's Spiced Rum Ice Cream Floats
directions: Add ice cream to your cup pour Rum and Soda over slowly squeeze the lime and garnish.  You can adjust the amout of rum to taste.  If done right it should taste like cream soda. Is also a great dink without the ice cream.

name: Champagne Jello Shots
directions: Line a 13 x 9 baking pan with nonstick foil.
have ready any shape cookie cutters.
put flavored jello in a meduim bowl set aside.
sprinkle unflavored gelatin over fruit juice in a small saucepan
let stand 1 minute
stir over low heat 2-3 min till gelatin granules completely dissolve.
add to flavored jello stir 3 min till dissolved.
stir in champagne.
pour into baking pan and refrigerate 3 hours till firm
lift foil from pan using cookie cutters to cut shapes through gelatin
stack the different jello flavors in a cup alternating (if you have made two different fllavors of jello, meaning making two separate batches).

name: Watermelon Fizz
directions: Put your watermelon in the cup and crush it up a bit with your fork, releasing some juices and making the slices smaller. I'd use smaller slices of watermelon.
Pour in your bottle of Mike's Hard Lemonade.
Stir slightly, sit back, and enjoy!

name: Orange Daiquiri
directions: In a cocktail shaker, combine rum, juices and simple syrup; shake well. Strain into 2 ice-filled old-fashion glasses. Garnish each with a twisted zest strip.

name: Sunset Ember
directions: For Grenadine:   Bring juice and sugar to a boil in a medium saucepan. Reduce heat and simmer to thicken slightly. let cool. Grendine will keep, covered and refrigerated, for up to 1 month.
For Cocktail: In a cocktail shaker, combine rum, juice, simple syrup,grenadine, and egg whites; shake well. Strain into 2 chilled cocktail glasses. Garnish each with a cherry.

name: Hard Raspberry Lemonade
directions: stir together raspberries, beer, thawed concentrate, and vodka.
Serve over ice.
Garnish if desired.
Note: To make ahead, stir raspberry lemonade concentrate and vodka in large container. Chill up to 8 days. Stir in raspberries and beer just before serving.

name: Low-Calorie Starbucks-Like Iced Chai Latte
directions: Boil water in kettle, and pour out into mug so that it is halfway filled.
Add 3 tsp of the Chai mix, dissolve. Add Splenda if desired.
Fill your drinking class to the brim with ice, and pour the chai tea into the glass.
Fill the rest of the glass up with milk and stir. Enjoy!

name: Guava Delight
directions: 1.  Mix all ingredients and serve chilled.

name: Godiva Martini
directions: Mix all ingredients in a shaker with ice, shake and pour into a chilled cocktail glass.

name: Kiwi - Watermelon Margarita Slush
directions: Make Lime Infused Simple Syrup:.
Bring 1 Cup Water to a boil and add Sugar.  Stir until sugar dissolves and remove from heat.   Stir in juice and zest of one lime.    Cool in refrigerator for 2-4 hours.
Make Watermelon Ice Cubes:.
Place Watermelon and 1/2 cup of the Lime Infused Simple Syrup into a blender.   Liquefy and pour into 2 ice cube trays.   Freeze until firm -- about 4 hours.
To make the Margarita:.

Add the Watermelon Cubes, Kiwi Cubes, Tequila, Orange Liquer, and Juice of the second lime into a blender.   Blend until the desired consistency.  I actually like it to be less frozen than a typical Margarita or Daquiri.    I also like to do this in batches as to not overload the blender.
Serve in pretty glasses rimmed with coarse salt.   Garnish with a splash of sparkling wine and kiwi slices.

name: Asian Pear Mojito
directions: Muddle the mint, juice from 3 lime wedges and sugar.
Add 2 Oz of Rum
Add the pear nectar
Add 8 ice cubes
Shake vigorously
Strain into the glass full of crushed ice
Top the drink with club soda
Add sprig of fresh mint, lime wedge and pear slices.

name: White Sangria
directions: Combine sugar, Calvados, lime, lemon, peaches and apples in a large pitcher.
Cover with 1 bottle of Rioja wine and chill sangria several hours.
To serve, spoon fruits into large glasses and pour wine over top of the fruit.
Top glasses of sangria off with a splash of soda water and serve.

name: Acapulco Cliff Hanger
directions: Make the simple syrup: combine water and sugar in a sauce pan; stir to mix; place over med-high heat to dissolve; as soon as the syrup comes to a rolling boil and is completely clear, remove from heat.
Add in the lemons (keep them submerged by placing a plate on top of them during the infusing process); cover and let steep for 30-40 minutes.
Pour the syrup through a fine sieve, pressing down firmly on the lemons to extract all the juice into the syrup; store in a covered container in the refrigerator for up to 1 month.
Make the drink: puree the watermelon in the blender; strain, for about 1/3 cup puree.
In a tall mixing glass, combine the watermelon puree, tequila, lemon simple syrup, lime juice, and Cointreau; stir to mix.

Moisten the rim of the serving glass with a lime wedge and press the rim into spiced salt (combine 1 teaspoon kosher salt, a generous pinch of cayenne pepper, and a small pinch of ground ginger); fill glass with ice.
Strain the puree mixture into the cocktail glass; top with a splash of soda water or ginger ale, if you wish.
Squeeze the lime wedge on top; garnish with small cubes watermelon.

name: Kinky Russian
directions: Pour the coffee liqueur and the coconut rum over ice cubes in an old-fashioned glass.
Fill with half and half cream, stir, and serve.

name: Gilligan's Dream Drink Recipe
directions: Combine all ingredients in blender. Blend on high speed until smooth.  Immediately pour mixture into two Margarita glasses, dividing equally. Garnish with tropical fruits, little paper umbrellas, and enjoy!

name: Moscow Mule
directions: Add vodka, sweetened lime juice, and bitters to a cocktail shaker filled with ice.  Shake vigorously until condensation forms on outside of shaker (about 15 to 20 seconds).  Strain into a collins glass (or copper mug if you have it) and top off with ginger ale.  Stir.  Garnish with a lime wedge and serve.

name: Spiked Firefly Palmer
directions: Mix together in a tall glass with ice.

name: Sweet Tea Ginger Glow
directions: Pour in a tall glass over ice, garnish with lemon twist.

name: Raspberry Limoncello Martini
directions: Add ingredients into cocktail shaker filled with ice; shake vigorously.
Strain into martini glass.

name: Lemon Cream Soda
directions: Spoon 1/4 cup lemon curd into each of 4 pint sized dessert glasses.
Add 1/4 sparkling water and stir to combine.
Add 2 scoops of ice cream to each glass; top with additional sparkling water.
Serve immediately with straws.

name: Mexican Hot Chocolate
directions: Blend raw almonds until even and fine.  Add water and dates and blend again.  Strain liquid. (OR use 8 cups of almond milk).  Add water to make 8 cups.
Bring almond milk and cinnamon to a boil.
Simmer and stir in cocoa and sugar.
Add cayenne pepper, vanilla and rum right before serving.
***If you have time, you can soak the almonds and dates in water overnight and then blend for a deeper flavor.  Add water to make 8 cups.

name: Coffee Syrup
directions: combine coffee & sugar & boil them until they resemble thick gravy.
when serving put 2 teaspoonfuls of the syrup & fill the rest of the cup with hot water,then stir well.

name: Thai Lychee Soda
directions: Puree the lychee fruit and water until a smooth consistency is reached.
Strain the mixture into a pitcher, and discard the pulp.
add lime juice and red syrup and stir well.
pour into glasses filled with ice cubes until 3/4 full.
top the rest off with club soda.
Garnish with mint and serve.

name: Pink Lemonade Raspberry Firefly Zinger
directions: Pour in a tall glass filled with ice, add lemon twist for garnish.

name: Watermelon Nectar
directions: Cut the flesh of a seedless watermelon into chunks, and puree them in batches in a blender.
Strain the fruit through a sieve (discard solids), and chill before serving.

name: Best Mint Cocoa
directions: Put everything into a slow cooker and cook on low stirring regularly for 1 hour.

name: German Chocolate Martini
directions: Combine Godiva Original Liqueur, Captain Morgan Parrot Bay Coconut Rum, and Smirnoff Black Cherry Vodka.
Shake with ice and strain into martini glass.
Garnish with chocolate shavings.

name: Chef Joey's Organic Hemp Milk
directions: Fill the cup of a large blender with 7 cups of water.
Add the hemp seed, honey and vanilla.
Blend for a few minutes to ensure the seeds are all broken up.
Taste your milk.  If its not sweet enough or flavored enough, add more sweetener or vanilla.  You can also add almond extract as well.

If you are happy with the milk let it sit for a few minutes to let the water get into the seed puree.  This step is important because it will help plump the seeds up and prevent them from leaking out into the milk while you're straining it.
Once the milk is strained put in the fridge.
Drink it up quickly this stuff won't keep too long.
Bon Appetit!

name: Absolut Crimson Pears
directions: Fill a shaker with ice cubes. Add all ingredients. Shake and strain into a chilled cocktail glass.

name: Peary Afternoon
directions: Fill a shaker with ice cubes. Add all ingredients. Shake and strain into a chilled highball glass filled with ice cubes. Garnish with melon slice.

name: Dawn's Mulled Wine
directions: Put red wine, orange pieces, cinnamon sticks & bay leaf in a pan.
Warm but do not boil. Keep warm for 5 – 10 mins to let flavours infuse.
When warm add brown sugar to required sweetness.
Add the brandy or rum for a little ‘oomph’.

name: Sugar Free Amaretto
directions: Combine water and sugars in a very large saucepan over medium heat.
Heat until the mixture is boiling and all sugar is dissolved.
Reduce heat to low and continue to simmer for approximately 2 hours until mixture is thickened and syrupy and lightly coats a spoon.
Remove pan from heat and allow to cool for 10 minutes.
Add vodka and extracts and stir well.
Pour into a bottle and seal.

name: Diet Iced Tea
directions: Steep teabags in boiling water for 20 minutes. Add Splenda & Crystal Light & stir well. Fill pitcher with water & stir. After tea is cool, refrigerate.

name: Cranberry Orange Jell-O Shots
directions: Pour jell-o into a large bowl.  Add 1 cup boiling water and stir until gelatin is dissolved.  Stir in vodka and divide between twenty 1 oz disposable plastic cups.  Refrigerate until firm.

name: Flavored Mocha Drink Mix
directions: In a bowl, combine the first five ingredients. Divide mixture in half. Stir in vanilla to one portion and almond extract to the other. Store in airtight containers in a cool dry place for up to 1 year. Yield: 14-16 batches (7-8 batches vanilla mocha mix; 7-8 batches almond mocha mix), about 3 cups total.
To prepare beverage: Dissolve about 3 tablespoons mix in water; stir well. Top with whipped cream if desired. Yield: 1 serving per batch.

name: Ally's Low-Cal Margarita
directions: rub lime juice along the rim of the glass and dip into a shallow dish of salt or your favorite low calorie sweetener.
Mix all ingredients together and pour over 1 cup of crushed ice.
Optional:  may garnish with a lime slice.

similar.name: Hungry Girl's  Magical Low Calorie Margarita

name: Hot Toddy
directions: Put bourbon, honey, and lemon juice in a 6-ounce mug. Top off with hot water and stir until honey is dissolved.

similar.name: Dr. Pat's Hot Toddy Cold Remedy
Dr. Joey's Hot Toddy's Cold Remedy

name: Peachtree Martini
directions: Mix over ice in a cocktail shaker.
Strain into a chilled martini glass.

name: Cabana Club
directions: Blend with crushed rice.

name: Sake Tini
directions: Shake Shake Shake over ice until frosty cold.

name: Strawberry Cosmo
directions: Mix vodka and juice, squeeze lime wedge into drink and garnish with ice cubes.

name: Kurant Cosmopolitan
directions: HALF-FILL A COCKTAIL SHAKER WITH ICE and add the vodka, cranberry juice, Rose's lime juice, and Triple Sec.
Cover the shaker and shake vigorously until well chilled (a precise 14 shakes at Adam's Place).
STRAIN THE COCKTAIL into a martini glass, garnish the rim with the lime slice, and serve right away.

name: Pungent Spice-Infused Hot Chocolate
directions: In a small saucepan, combine the milk, cinnamon and cloves  over low heat.
Simmer very slowly for 10 minutes; remove the cinnamon stick and whole cloves.
Add in the chocolate and stir continuously with a wooden spoon until completely melted.
Add in sugar to taste, stir until dissolved.
Remove the mixture from the heat and allow to steep for 10 minutes.
Return to the stove over low heat and gently return to a simmer before serving.

name: Montparnasse
directions: Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled Martini glass. Garnish by floating a wafer-thin apple slice.

name: Pink Vodka Cocktail
directions: combine the triple sec and vodka in a jug. Divide the mixture among eight 60ml (1/4 cup) capacity glasses.
Gradually add 1 teaspoon of grenadine to the centre of each glass so the grenadine sinks to the bottom of the glass.
Serve Immediatley.

name: Coffee Concentrate
directions: Mix the coffee and the water.  Leave it on the counter overnight. Strain in the morning, preferably through cheesecloth.

For hot coffee just add hot water to taste to 1/4 cup (or more) of the concentrate mixture (depending on how strong - or not - you like your coffee).  To make iced coffee, stir together 1/4 cup each of Coffee Concentrate and water. If desired, stir in milk and sugar. Serve over ice.
It should keep for 2-3 weeks in the fridge.

name: Stevia Lemonade
directions: Just mix together -- remember that stevia is super sweet and you don't need a lot.  I never use more than 2 drops.  Play around until it works for you.

name: Madras Cocktail
directions: Add vodka and cranberry juice to a high ball glass filled with ice, stir to combine.
Float orange juice on top.
Enjoy.

name: Creamy Hot White Chocolate
directions: In a large saucepan, combine 1/2 cup cream, vanilla chips, cinnamon sticks, cinnamon and nutmeg. Cook and stir over low heat until chips are melted. Stir in remaining cream; heat through. Discard cinnamon sticks. Stir in vanilla.

name: Homemade Sweet-And-Sour Mix
directions: Combine water and sugar in a saucepan; bring to a boil, stirring, until sugar dissolves; cool syrup.
Mix syrup with lemon and lime juices; chill until cold; keep covered in refrigerator for up to one week.
Note: This mixture is great for most cocktails, but for margaritas you may want to reduce the sugar to 1/2 cup.

name: Copycat Olive Garden Sangria
directions: First, make the simple syrup by boiling the water and sugar.  I usually boil them for about 15-20 minutes while stirring constantly.  Allow this to cool to room temperature.
Stir the wine, sweet vermouth, grenadine, cranberry juice and simple syrup together.  Add a slice of orange to the glass to make it pretty.  Then add a splash of 7-Up right before serving.
Repeat all night!

name: Ww Spicy Hot Cocoa
directions: Bring 3 cups of water to boil.
Mix cocoa mix, vanilla, nutmeg, and ginger, cinnamon, brown sugar, and condensed milk in a heatproof pitcher.
Add hot water and stir to blend.
Serve in 4 mugs topped with 1/4 cup of whipped cream each.

name: Matcha  Hot Chocolate
directions: In a heavy saucepan over medium heat, warm the half-and-half, stirring often to prevent sticking, until it is very hot.
Remove from heat and sprinkle in the matcha and then the white chocolate.
Let white chocolate melt, about 1 minute.
Using an immersion blender, mix until thoroughly combined and the liquid is frothy.
Pour into serving cups and serve immediately.

name: Hot Chocolaty Cocoa
directions: Microwave: Mix all ingredients. Heat in microwave for 30 second, stir. Continue heating and stirring until boiling. Let cool to your desired temperature and enjoy.
Stovetop: Mix all ingredients. Stir constantly, heat over medium/low heat until boiling. Let cool to your desired temperature and enjoy.

Options: Top with marshmallows or whip-cream** (should you diet permit). And don’t be shy! If you love chocolate, dribble a few chips on top for an extra chocolaty bite. It makes a pretty decoration too (any excuse for chocolate is good)!

name: Chip Shot
directions: Mix the first three ingredients, top with whipped cream.  Hold onto your britches cuz this will sneak up on you.

name: Apple Cider Mulling Packets
directions: Cut cheese cloth into 8 6 inch square pieces.
Mix together top 5 ingredients.    Divide the mixture evenly amongst the square cheese cloth squares.   Tie with twine.
Use sachet to season in a mug or pot of warm cider.

name: Littlemafia's Apple Cider
directions: Mix together and boil for 10 minutes, then remove cinnamon sticks and cloves.

name: Homemade Root Beer (Rootbeer); Mccormick
directions: Clean and sanitize all bottling equipment according to the information below.
Mix contents of bottle with 5 pounds of sugar in a large container.  (DO NOT USE ALUMINUM).  Stir in 5 gallons lukewarm spring water (approximately 95*F).
Dissolve yeast in 1 Celsius pre-boiled water cooled to 85-95*F.  Allow to dissolve undisturbed 10-15 minutes.  Add to sugar mixture and STIR WELL.

Bottle immediately into plastic bottles, leaving 2-inch head space at top of bottle.  Cap tightly.  Store bottles on their side in a warm place (70-80*F) for 1-2 days, then store upright in the refrigerator 40-45*F for 3-4 days more.  Keep chilled and consume within 7-8 days.
CLEANING INFORMATION:  Before using, sanitize bottles and equipment ina mild bleach solution, 2 oz. per 5 gallons cold water.  Rinse several times with tap water inside and out.

name: Homemade Root Beer (Rootbeer)
directions: Stir together water and sugar in a 5-gallon beverage container.
Stir in root beer extract.
Add 1.5 pounds dry ice and add more as needed.  Don't add all at once, because it will freeze the bottom few inches of root beer (found this out by mistake :)  !)
After about 15 minutes, the root beer will be carbonated and ready to go.

name: Lavender Lemonade
directions: Bring 1 cup water to a boil in a medium saucepan. Combine the boiling water and lavender in a medium bowl; cover and steep 30 minutes.
Strain the lavender mixture through a fine sieve into a bowl; discard lavender leaves.
Combine 3 cups water and sugar in saucepan. Bring the mixture to a boil, and cook 1 minute or until sugar is dissolved.
Combine lavender water, sugar syrup, and lemon juice in a pitcher. Cover and chill. Serve over ice. Garnish lemonade with lavender stems, if desired.

name: Rose Sangria With Pineapple and Guava
directions: Cut pineapple into 3/4 " pieces, cut oranges into slices.
Set aside 2 orange slices
Put all ingredients into a large glass pitcher.
Let chill in the refrigerator overnight.
Fill tumblers with ice pour sangria over the ice and garnish with orange slices.

name: Easy Pitcher of Margaritas
directions: Put all of the ingredients in a pitcher.  Use the empty limeade can to measure the water (2 1/2 cans) and tequila (1 can).
Stir to mix.  I don't put ice in the pitcher since they will water down.
Serve with or without a salted rim over ice or frozen.
Any leftovers can be kept in the fridge.

name: Mcdonald's Vanilla Iced Coffee
directions: Brew coffee.
When coffee is done brewing, you may place it in the freezer for 15 minutes to cool it down a little so less ice will be melted.
Fill 22 ounce cup to top with ice.
Add syrup and half & half to cup.
Fill cup rest of way to top with coffee.
Mix carefully but thoroughly with spoon.
Add straw.
Enjoy!

name: Moscow Bobsled (Shot, Drink, You Decide)
directions: mix together and drink!

name: Bubblegum Martini
directions: Mix in shaker with 1-2 ice cubes, strain into chilled martini glass.  Garnish with a maraschino cherry.

name: Strawberry Basil Lemonade [with Citrus Vodka]
directions: Combine lemonade, strawberries, and basil leaves in a small container, stirring well.
Add vodka.
Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours.
To serve, remove basil leaves (if they're too wilted), and serve with ice and fresh basil leaves.

name: Strawberry Basil Lemonade
directions: Mix vodka and puree in a mixing glass or martini shaker.
Add ice, and shake for 15 seconds and pour into a glass.
Fill with lemonade and stir.
Add basil leaves and enjoy!

name: Reeses Martini
directions: pour the hershy coca mix on a plate.
pour hershy chocolate sauce on a plate.
pour the peanut butter creme liquor and the chocolate vodka in a shaker and shake well.
to rim the top of the glass spin the top in the chocolate sauce.
dip in hershy coca mix.
pour martini in glass.

name: Spicy Lust
directions: Chill a martini glass.
In a cocktail shaker add a scoop of crushed ice, vodka, and tabasco sauce.  Shake hard and fast.  Immediately strain into the martini glass.
Garnish with the jalapeno pepper to serve.
Don't be a sissy and enjoy!

name: Purple Nurple
directions: Fill a highball glass half way with crushed ice.
Combine the vodka, grape juice, and ginger ale in the glass.  Stir to mix and garnish with the grapes.
Drink up!

name: Wake up Cowboy!
directions: Mix together the ingredients and divide evenly between two coffee mugs.   Serve to your favorite cowboy with breakfast.

name: Angry Red Planet
directions: Salt the rim of a highball glass and chill.
Combine the ginger, wasabi, garlic, soy, and pepper in a bowl and muddle into a paste.    It will be slightly lumpy.
Stir in the vodka.
Pour mixture in a large mixing glass or cocktail shaker along with tomato juice and lime juice.   Mix well.
Fill the chilled glass with ice and add the vodka mixture.
Garnish with the lime wedge.  Enjoy!

name: Cherry-Limeade
directions: Half fill a highball glass with crushed ice.  Add the vodka, cherry juice, and lime juice.  Stir and splash top with the sprite.     Serve with the cherries and enjoy!

name: Sipping Chocolate
directions: Combine milk, MILKY WAY® Bars, and cinnamon in saucepan and warm over medium heat, stirring. Once hot and well blended, remove from heat and add vodka and almond-flavored liqueur.
Serve in tall mugs with a dollop of whipped cream and a sprinkling of shaved DOVE® Milk Chocolate, if you like.

name: No Added Sugar - Cranberry Pineapple Iced Tea
directions: Add pineapple juice + 3 cans of water to iced tea, stir.
Add cranberry juice + 3 cans of water to iced tea, stir.
Enjoy!

name: Classic Sidecar
directions: Combine all ingredient in a cocktail shaker and shake.
Strain mixture into a stemmed cocktail glass with a sugared rim.

name: Toasted Coconut Colada
directions: Blend everything with 9 large ice cubes; process until smooth.
Top with additional coconut and pineapple.

name: Summertime Iced Tea
directions: Steep teabags in boiling water for 5 minutes. Discard teabags & add other ingredients. Stir well. Do not refrigerate until it is cool.

name: Prosecco Splash
directions: Spoon 3 tablespoons pomegranate liqueur into each of 18 champagne flutes.
Pour Prosecco into each flute, filling 2/3 full.
Drop a frozen blackberry and raspberry into each glass; serve immediately.

name: Malted Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together and keep in an airtight container.
Use 3 tablespoons of mix per each cup of hot milk.

name: Cafe Caribbean
directions: The sugar and whipped cream can be replaced with one big scoop of good ice cream.
Mix Amaretto, rum and coffee,Add sugar.
Top with whipped cream.

name: Monkey's Gland Cocktail
directions: Shake the gin, orange juice, grenadine, and Pernod very well with ice.
Strain into a chilled cocktail glass.
Garnish with orange peel.

name: Lynds-Gria
directions: Mix all in a pitcher. May add fruit, frozen makes great ice replacement.

name: Vampire Kiss Martini
directions: In a shaker filled with ice, combine vodka and juice.
Strain into martini glass and garnish with lemon twist.

name: 'gin'-Ger Basiltini
directions: Place the cucumber and gin in a clean glass jar.  Seal the lid tightly and set aside for 5 days, shaking once a day. (I did 4 days). Strain the gin through a few layers of cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer into the original gin bottle.  Label the bottle and refrigerate before using.

In a medium saucepan, combine the water and sugar over high heat.  Boil for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally until the sugar is completely dissolved.  Remove from heat and add the ginger, allowing it to steep until the mixture is fully cool.  Strain the syrup through cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer into a clean glass jar.  This makes 1 cup ginger simple syrup.  Cover and refrigerate.  It will keep for 2 weeks.

In the bottom of a cocktail shaker, muddle together 5 basil leaves with the lime juice.  Fill the shaker with ice, then add 1 1/2 ounces cucumber infused gin and 1/2 ounce simple syrup.  Shake the ingredients vigorously, then strain into a chilled cocktail glass.  Garnish with the remaining basil leaf and serve immediately.

name: Debbies White Peach Sangria
directions: Place all ingredients in a pitcher and stir to mix.
Refrigerate at least 8 hours.
Serve over ice.

name: Kahlua and Brandy Coffee
directions: Add the brandy and Kahlua to the hot coffee.
Quickly top with whipped cream.

name: Kahlua and Rum Coffee
directions: Moisten the top edge of a coffee cup with a little rum.
Place a little sugar on a dish, and dip the cup into the sugar to coat rim. Add the rum and the Kahlua, and mix together.
Add the hot coffee, and stir quickly to mix.
Top with whipped cream.
The whipped cream prevents the alcohol from escaping.

name: Eggnog Liqueur
directions: Using the blunt edge of your knife, scrape the insides from the vanilla bean.
Add the vanilla scrapings to the blender.  Add remaining ingredients and blend for about 45 seconds on high, or until thoroughly combined and smooth.
Pour into a clean bottle with a tight fitting lid or a clean canning jar with a tight fitting lid.

Place in the refrigerator and allow it to mellow for at least two weeks prior to serving.  The longer it ages, the smoother the flavor will be.  Another little bit of magic; as it ages, it will deepen not only in flavor but in color.  If you use eggs from your own chickens -or from the chickens of a generous farmer friend- the liqueur will reach an almost orange color.
This is good for two months in the refrigerator.

*Take your scraped vanilla bean pod and put it in a jar with a couple cups of plain sugar.  Put a tight lid on it and stick it in a dark place.  In a week you’ll have vanilla sugar.  As you use it up and start running low, just add additional sugar and shake the jar.  Voila!  Never ending vanilla sugar!

name: Double Coffee Iced Latte
directions: Combine chilled espresso*, milk and Torani in a tall glass filled with ice and stir well.
*If needed, 1/2 cup strong brewed coffee can be substituted for 1/4 cup espresso.

name: Mint Tulips
directions: Heat water & pour over mint leaves. Dissolve sugar in hot water. Steep 30 minutes to 2 hours. Strain out leaves. Add lemon juice & ginger ale when ready to serve. Pour over ice with a sprig of mint in each glass.

name: Warm Cough and Flu Remedy
directions: In a saucepan over medium heat, combine the water, lemon juice, ginger, lemon zest, cinnamon and cloves and bring to a boil.  Once it is boiling, reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat, add honey and molasses, stir thoroughly, allow to steep 10 minutes.
Strain and serve warm or hot.

Strain remainder into a jar and refrigerate, heat to serve. I advise against leaving the the ginger and spices in the liquid as it is quite strong and spicy, but there is no harm in leaving them doing so and straining before consuming. You might want to add some extra honey or dilute it a bit, however, as the spices will have intensified.

name: Hot Spiced Tea
directions: Bring water to a boil. Pour over tea bag, cinnamon stick and cloves in a teapot; cover and steep 5 minutes. Remove tea bag, squeezing gently. Stir Splenda, orange juice and lemon into tea. Pour tea through a wire mesh strainer into cups, discarding spices.

To Make 4 Servings: Bring 4 cups water to a boil. Pour over 3 regular-size tea bags, 2 cinnamon sticks and 1/2 teaspoon whole cloves in a teapot; cover and steep 5 minutes. Remove tea bags, squeezing gently. Stir 12 packets Splenda, 1/4 cup orange juice and 1 1/2 teaspoons fresh lemon juice into tea. Pour tea through a wire mesh strainer into cups, discarding spices.

name: Sexy Chocolate Russian
directions: Begin by heating up the 1/2 cup of hot chocolate (1/2 cup water packet of cocao, simple).
When hot to the touch add the liquors.
Stir slowly.
Enjoy with care!

name: Homemade Ginger Ale
directions: Place the ginger, lemon rind, sugar, and homey in a bowl and add the boiling water. Cool, refrigerate for 8 hours or overnight, and strain. When ready to serve, add the club soda.

name: Organic Ginger Juice
directions: Peel the ginger.
Cut it up into 1/2 inch pieces.
Put it in your blender with the water and process until the ginger is totally broken down and you have a frothy yellow juice.
Then get yourself a large bowl and a fine metal kitchen seive with a handle and pour the ginger juice into it.
Using a laddel slowly press the ginger mixture through the sieve getting all the liquid out.
Disgard the ginger pulp.
Store in the fridge in a tightly sealed jar.
Add to your favorite fruit drinks.
Bon Appetit!

name: Wild Blueberry Mimosa
directions: In a champagne flute, add one ounce of wild blueberry juice.  Pour champagne on top and garnish with fresh or frozen blueberries.

name: Wild Blueberry Martini
directions: Add all ingredients to crushed ice.  Shake, strain and pour into a chilled martini glass.  Garnish with a skewer of blueberries.

name: R L M's Ginger Ale
directions: Place the peeled & chopped ginger root in a good blender along with the sugar.  Add the hot water and cover.  Blend on high for two minutes.
Let the mixture rest for an hour.
Filter the mixture, pressing with the back of a spoon through a sieve.
Refrigerate liquid overnight in an air tight container.
The next day add the lemon extract and stir gently to blend.
Take two clean 1 liter bottles and divide the ginger liquid equally between them.
Divide the sparkling water between the two bottles, gently shake. and store in your refrigerator until you are ready to drink.

name: Raspberry Whip
directions: Mix Splenda sweetener (use the flavor accents that come in the stick that can just be poured into a drink) and cool whip in a small bowl.
Place ice in a tall clear glass, pour diet soda into glass.
Top with cool whip mixture.

name: Caramel Apple Protein Cider
directions: Combine all ingredients together and mix well and store in an airtight container. When you're ready to make a mug of cider, here's how:

Add 1/2 cup of mixture to mug. Add a splash of cold water and mix well until you have a smooth paste with no lumps. Slowly add hot water and continue mixing to eliminate any lumps. I have large mugs so I used about 10-12oz of water per batch. If you have smaller mugs, then reduce the amount of dry mix.

If you want to mix your drink the "automated" way (like I do), here's how. Add 1/2c mixture to a blender jar and add the splash of cold water. Turn on the blender on low and let it mix and then slowly add hot water from the tea kettle while the blender is still whirling. Pour into a mug and enjoy.

similar.name: Spiced Apple Cider With Rum Whipped Cream

name: Easy Eggnog
directions: In a large bowl, whisk 3/4 cup milk and pudding mix until smooth. Whisk in the sugar, vanilla, spices. Stir in the remaining milk.
Refrigerate.

name: Watermelon Lemonade
directions: Stir it all together and serve over ice. Garnish it with a thin watermelon or lemon wedge, if you’re feeling fancy.

similar.name: Honey Lemonade With Frozen Fruit Cubes

name: Pumpkin Spice Martini
directions: Pour the vanilla flavored vodka, Irish cream liquer and pumkin flavored lqueur.

into a cocktail shaker over ice. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into a chilled martini glass and garnish with a sprinkle of ground cinnamon  and ground nutmeg to serve.

name: Rocky Mountain Ginger Crisp by Cheri Loughlin
directions: Combine ingredients in a mixing glass.
Stir.
Pour into a highball glass filled with ice.
Garnish with a crystallized ginger round.

name: Yummy Sweet Tea
directions: Put sugar, lemon juice and tea bags  in 2 quart pitcher.
Bring water to boil in tea kettle.
Pour boiling water into 2 quart container with other ingredients.
Place lid on 2 quart pitcher and allow tea to steep for 5 minutes.
Remove tea bags and add cold water until it is full to the 2 quart line.
Mix well add ice and serve.

name: Spiced Lemon Tea
directions: In a medium pan on stove put water, cloves and cinnamon; bring to a boil.  Remove from heat and add tea bags; steeping for 5 minutes.
Disscard tea bags from pot and stir in lemon juice and brown sugar.  Return to heat and warm through (do not boil again).
Strain and divide between 2 mugs and enjoy!

name: Drink Your Tiramisu!
directions: Between 2 mugs mix espresso with golden sugar and amaretto.
In a small bowl blend the mascarpone, milk, vanilla and super fine sugar until smooth.
Using the BACK of a WARM, DRY teaspoon held over the mug, pour the mascarpone mix slowly into the mug so it floats on top of the espresso.
Sprinkle with chocolate flakes and serve with lady finger cookies! YUM!
*Note: for a non-alcoholic version, replace the amaretto with almond extract.

name: Hazelnut Coffee Sparkler
directions: Chill coffee in fridge for approximately 45 min's.
In a blender pour chilled coffee, soda water, Frangelico, and brown sugar.  Whirl until mixed.
Add ice cubes and blend till smooth.
*Note #1: for a non-alcoholic drink use hazelnut syrup.
*Note #2: for a grown up soda float, add a little scoop of ice cream!

name: Hot Shot Cinnamon Overload
directions: HOT:.
Brew the coffee.
Place an ounce of the Goldschlager liqueur into each cup.
Fill the cups with the hot coffee.
Add sugar or Splenda if using.
Place the long cinnamon sticks into the cups to to stir with (leave them in the cups or remove as you wish).
ICED:.
In advance brew a pot the same coffee that you’ll be using the make the cinnamon coffee and freeze it in an ice tray, save two cups of the coffee.
Place the saved two cups of coffee into the refrigerator to chill.
When your ready to make the iced drink fill each of two glasses half full of the coffee cubes.
Pour an ounce of Goldschlager over the ice.
Add the chilled coffee to each glass and sweeten if you wish.
Place the long cinnamon sticks into the glasses to stir and mix with.

name: Fresh Pomegranate Martini
directions: 1. CHILL your large martini glass.
2. Use a hand juicer ( the same type that you would use for citrus)--and juice the 1/2 pomegranate.
3. Mix juice, vodka and raspberry liquer.
4. Shake over ice.
5. Pour into chilled glass.

name: Homemade Limoncello
directions: Combine vodka and zest in a half gallon jar and let stand at room temperature. tightly covered, for about 10 days.  It is ready when the zest has turned pale and vodka is a deep yellow.
Pour liquid through a strainer into a large bowl.  Leave zest in the strainer and place stainer over a large measuring cup.

Bring 3 cups of water and sugar to boil over high heat, stirring  until the sugar is dissolved.  Boil for 3 minutes.  Pour syrup through strainer into measuring cup and discard the zest.  Cool syrup completely.

Add syrup to vodka.  Pour liqueur into a large bottle or smaller decorative bottles with tight fitting lids.  Let stand for 5 days before serving. Store in the fridge and serve ice cold in chilled shot glasses.  If you store homemade limoncello in the freezer it may freeze.  (although the friend that gave me this recipe says that is where she stores hers).

name: Mexican Frappatini
directions: Pour all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake well. Serve over ice or strain into martini glasses.

name: Stoli Honey Bee (Cocktail)
directions: HONEY SYRUP:.
In tiny saucepan, heat honey and water together until a syrup consistency. Remove from heat and allow to cool.
FINISH:.
Add honey syrup, vodka, and lemon juice to a cocktail shaker filled with crushed ice. Shake well and serve in a martini glass.
Garnish with a lemon twist.

name: Sarasota's Warm Apple/Cranberry/Orange Spiced Cocktail
directions: Base  --  In a big pot, just mix on medium low heat the orange juice, apple cider, cranberry juice, honey, cinnamon sticks, whole cloves and orange strips. Note, if using your fresh oranges for your orange juice, peel off the orange strips before you squeeze your orange, it is so much easier.  I just took a knife and peeled a few long strips.

Simmer  --  On medium low heat for about 25 minutes uncovered. This can also go into a crock pot and cooked 1 hour on high or med for about 2 hours and low for about 3, just until warm.  All crock pots are a bit different, so just check until nice and hot.

Serve  --  Take a strainer or slotted spoon to remove the cloves and cinnamon sticks from the cider.  Then mix the brandy and rum in a measuring cup so you just pour a little into each cup as you serve them.  Add a few cranberries and a orange slice to the bottom of your mug, a good shot or so of the brandy/rum mixture and top with the cider. A great drink for the whole family.  ENJOY!

name: Green Jasmine-Mint Iced Tea With Lemon
directions: Steep loose tea (or tea bags) and mint leaves in simmering (not boliing) water for 2 to 3 minutes.
Strain the tea (or remove the tea bags and mint leaves) and pour into a large pitcher. Stir in lemon juice and honey until the honey is dissolved.
Add cold water. Refrigerate until chilled, about 2 hours.
Serve over ice with mint sprigs, if desired.

similar.name: Oak Alley's Sweet Mint Iced Tea

name: Iced Mint and Cucumber Gin
directions: Place all ingredients but mint in blender.
Blend till well combined.
Pour into glasses.
Top with mint leaves to serve.

name: Mexican Frappaccini
directions: Mix ingredients over ice, shake well. Serve on the rocks or strain into martini glasses.

name: Yummy Iced Tea
directions: Place your Baking Soda in the bottom of a gallon pitcher.
Throw the Tea Bags in and run your Hot Hot Tap Water into the pitcher over the Tea Bags, till about half full. Swirl pitcher to make sure all the bags get soaked with water.
Let sit for about 10-20 minutes, letting it get as dark and strong as you like it. I like mine to be almost black; it lightens up a bit once you add the rest of the water.
Then remove your tea bags and stir in your sugar till dissolved.
Add remaining Cold Tap Water until pitcher is full, stir.
Refrigerate and Enjoy over ice or alone!

name: Jenny Jenny's Smokin Sangria
directions: Mix water and sugar. Make a simple syrup. When sugar is melted add fruits (sueeze them alittle to extract juices) and let it cook down some.  When syrup is cooled strain out any fruit pieces .Mix simple syrup, triple sec and  wine to taste. Add more or less syrup depending on how sweet you like your sangria. The recipe calls for equal parts but you may not like it that sweet. Add sliced fruits. Serve over ice and enjoy!

name: Peach Lassi
directions: Puree the canned peaches in a blender.
Add the yogurt and sugar and process until blended.
Finally, add the ice and process until smooth and frothy.
Enjoy!

similar.name: Yogurt Lassi With  Sweet or Savory Variations

name: Coffee With Mint
directions: Add all the ingredients in the coffee filter.
Add 6 cups of water.
Serve hot with whipped cream .

name: Matt's Caffeine Free Iced Raspberry Tea
directions: 1. Bring the water to a boil.
2. Reduce heat to a simmer and mix in sugar.  Make sure it's completely dissolved.
3. Begin steeping all tea bags.  Steap approximately 8-10 minutes or till desired flavor is achieved.
4. Allow to cool before serving and serve over ice.
5. Store unused tea in fridge.

name: Banana Hot Buttered Rum
directions: Divide the sugar, butter, rum and banana liqueur among 4 mugs. Top with boiling water, stir and garnish each drink with 1 or 2 banana slices.

name: Peppermint Hot Chocolate
directions: Place cocoa in a medium saucepan. Gradually whisk in milk until cocoa has dissolved.
Add chocolate chips and crushed peppermint; simmer over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until chocolate and peppermint have melted, 8 to 10 minutes.
Bring to a boil and immediately remove from heat. Add salt and vanilla (if desired). Ladle into mugs; serve each with a peppermint stick (or marshmallows).

name: Cafe Mocha Java from the Plaza Hotel
directions: Combine coffee and cocoa in a chafing dish.  Heat, but do not boil.
Ladle into coffee cups or mugs and top with whipped cream.
Coffee and chocolate together!

name: Santa's Hat
directions: Mix together powdered sugar and water until smooth.
Fold a paper towel into a 4-inch square and lay it on a saucer.
Spread the powdered sugar mixture on top of the towel on the saucer.
Spread coconut in a shallow layer on another saucer.
Dip the rim of a Martini glass in the sugar mixture on the towel until the rim is coated.
Then, dip the sugared rim into the coconut. Use a spoon to pack the coconut on to the sugar coating so it stays on the rim.
Add the cranberry juice, Malibu rum and grenadine into a cocktail shaker with ice.
Shake and strain into a prepared Martini glass and serve.
Garnish with a candy cane. Break off the top hook of the candy cane if desired.

name: Blueberry Lemonade (Alcoholic)
directions: Mix together and enjoy!

name: Applejack-Spiked Hot Cider
directions: Melt butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.  Stir in sugar and next 4 ingredients (through cinnamon sticks); cook 1 minute.
Add cider and next 4 ingredients (through lemon rind); bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to medium-low; simmer 15 minutes.  Remove from heat; stir in applejack.
Strain; discard solids.

name: Kahlua Root Beer Float
directions: Pour Kahlua and cola over ice cream in a tall glass. Fill with club soda.

name: Kahlua & Cognac
directions: Pour Kahlua and cognac or brandy in brandy snifter.
Swirl gently.

name: Kahlua Rum & Soda
directions: Pour Kahlua and rum over ice in a tall glass. Fill with club soda (I like Coke). Stir. Now relax.

similar.name: Cuba Libre (Better Known As Rum & Coke)

name: Kahlua & Soda
directions: Pour Kahlua over ice in a highball glass. Add club soda. Stir. Garnish with a lime wedge if desired.

name: Kahlua & Iced Tea
directions: Pour Kahlua and cooled or chilled tea over ice in a tall glass. Stir.

name: Homemade Amaretto
directions: Bring to boil water and sugars.
lower to a simmer and simmer 20 minutes.
cool to room temprature, then add extracts and stir.
add vodka stir again.
bottle and store in cool dark place.
let age 2 weeks, longer if you can wait.

name: Chipotle Chocolate
directions: Bring milk to a simmer in a saucepan over medium to medium-high heat.
Add in chocolate, chipotle powder, and cinnamon; whisk until the chocolate has melted.
Simmer for 1 minutes, whisking continuously.
Remove from heat, pour into mugs and serve.

name: Luxury Bloody Mary Mix
directions: In a large glass or plastic container, mix all ingredients; make sure the salt dissolves.
Cover and refrigerate, for up to 7 days.

name: Cinnamon Coffee
directions: Place coffee and cinnamon stick in the filter.On the coffee machine.
Serve with whatever you would normally.

name: Coquito (Chef Galarza's Family Recipe)
directions: I have given out this recipe to some friends and family over time. All of them have said the same thing theirs does not taste like mine. The truth about being a great cook is you have to love to cook or entertain, if you are just doing it to do it, it will not taste right. Do it because you have passion for it.
My personal spin on this goes like this:
1. Get your blender setup and start by pouring (1) can of evaporated milk, and adding (3) tbs of cinnamon. Pulse the blender for about 3 seconds.
2. Pour in (1) can of condensed milk and pulse for 5 seconds.
3. Pour in (1) can of evaporated milk and pulse for 5 seconds.
4.  Pour in (1) can of cream of coconut and pulse for 5 seconds.
5.  Pour in last can of condensed milk (3) tbs of vanilla and add the (4) oz of rum. Mix in blender for about 1 minute.

This should render about 2 liters of coquito. You can use sterilized empty wine bottles or purchase one. Pour into bottles or bottle and freeze for about (4) hours. This drink will not freeze completely, it will acquire the form of a melted ice cream. It is very rich, that is why it should be consumed as cold as possible. Enjoy.

name: Peach-Bourbon Sours
directions: Whisk together peach preserves and hot water in a glass pitcher until preserves are dissolved. Whisk in bourbon and lemon juice.

Fill a cocktail shaker with ice. Add one-third of bourbon mixture; cover with lid, and shake until throughly chilled. Pour over ice into 2 (8-12oz) glasses. Repeat procedure with remaining bourbon mixture. Garnish, if desired. Serve drinks immediately.

Quick Ideas: Add garnishes to match or complement the preserves flavor you choose. Fresh blackberries and mint sprigs look great on glasses of Blackberry-Bourbon Sours, while lemon and kiwifruit slices are fancy touches to Ginger-Bourbon Cocktails.

name: Flavored Simple Syrups
directions: Spiced Apple Simple Syrup: Bring sugar, apple juice, and apple pie spice to a boil in a saucepan. Cook 2 minutes or until sugar dissolves. Remove from heat,
and stir in ice cubes; freeze 20 minutes.

Cranberry-Orange Simple Syrup: Bring sugar, cranberries, orange juice, and water to a boil in a medium saucepan. Cook 2 minutes or until sugar dissolves. Remove from heat, and pour through a wire-mesh strainer, pressing berries with back of a spoon. Discard solids. Stir in ice cubes; freeze 20 minutes.

name: Kahlua Banana Cream Fizz
directions: In a blender, combine all the ingredients except soda.
Blend smooth.
Pour into 10 oz. glasses. Add club soda.
Garnish with banana chunks and cherries, if desired. (I skipped this step).
Makes 2 drinks or one nice big one.

name: Ginger Tea or Tisane
directions: Wash the ginger.
Peel the ginger and slice it thinly, in small pieces.
Boil the water.
There are three ways to perform the next step - the method will depend on your preference:

Pour the boiled water into a teapot that you have already placed the freshly sliced ginger into. Put the lid on the teapot to stop the pot cooling down too quickly and to keep the aromatic ingredients in the tea. Steep for 10 - 15 minutes.
If you boil the water in a saucepan rather than in a kettle, you can add the ginger to the saucepan and simmer for 15 - 20 minutes. Let it sit for 5 minutes before pouring after simmering.
Use a teaball to hold the sliced ginger and steep in a cup for 15 minutes. Make sure to use a saucer to cover the top of the cup to prevent the aromatic elements from escaping.
Strain the tea after steeping or boiling and serve. Add sweeteners or flavour if desired.
Drink hot, at room temperature or cold - as preferred.

name: Cranberry Cornucopia
directions: Bring the water to a boil in a big saucepan; remove from heat, add in the tea bags, and cover; steep for 5 minutes.
Remove the tea bags and add the brown sugar, cranberry juice, star anise, and orange zest.
Bring mixture to a boil over med-high heat.
Decrease heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add in orange juice and rum; stir until warm.
Remove from heat and strain, reserving the star anise.
Ladle into mugs; garnish with star anise and lemon slices; serve.

name: Nif's Easy Way to Flatten Ginger Ale (Sick Tummy Remedy)
directions: Pour ginger ale into a glass and stir in sugar. Watch the bubbles come up to the top and disappear!
Sip slowly!

name: Peppermint Mocha
directions: In a small saucepan, combine water and sugar. Bring to a boil. When sugar is dissolved, reduce to a simmer, add peppermint extract and simmer 20 minutes. (This makes enough for 6 drinks!).
In a warmed mug, combine cocoa mix and water, stirring to form a paste. Add the espresso, 1/2 Tbsp of peppermint syrup and the milk. Stir until well combined. Top with whipped cream if desired.

name: Pumpkin Spice Latte
directions: In a small saucepan, heat milk, pumpkin and sugar until sugar is dissolved and everything is steaming hot. Transfer to a blender with vanilla and pumpkin pie spice. Process until foamy, about 15 seconds. Pour into one large or two small mugs and top with espresso.

name: Irish Creme
directions: In very large bowl:.
Beat 16 eggs until uniform in texture & color.
Add the sweetened condensed milk to eggs and beat until well blended & uniform.
Add chocolate syrup to eggs and milk.
In separate bowl:.
Add 2 cups canadian mist and 9 tsp instant coffee together and blend until coffee is fully disolved.  Add to egg & milk mixture.
Add in remaining 10 cups of rye.
Add 16 cups of cream and beat until smooth and uniform.
Pour into individual 1/2 gallon or 1 gallon sized bottles and keep refrigerated.  Will keep for 3-4 weeks.

name: London Fog (My Low-Cost, Low-Fat Version)
directions: Place tea bag in a measuring cup and add boiling water, steep for at least 3min.
In your favourite mug, mix together honey, vanilla & milk.
Heat in microwave for 30 seconds.
Remove bag from tea and pour in your mug with the heated milk.
Stir. (If you have one of those mini frothers, froth the milk before adding the tea even better :-).
Enjoy. Careful, it's hot!

name: Pam's Whiskey Slush
directions: Bring the water and the sugar to a boil and let simmer for 15 minutes.
While the mixture is hot add the tea bags and steep.
Remove the tea bags and let the syrup cool.
When cool, add the frozen juices and alcohol and put in the freezer for a day or two.
To serve scoop the slush to fill a glass halfway and top with ginger ale.
Garnish with cherries and oranges if desired.

name: Creamsicle Coffee
directions: Brew coffee.  Pour into large mug.
Stir in syrups.
Add steamed milk to taste, with some foam on the top.

name: Hot Spiced Cider
directions: Stuff cored apple with cloves.
Put all ingredients in large pot.
Bring to boil and simmer for 15 minutes.
Put on low to keep warm for serving.

name: Hot Cranberry Punch
directions: Combine all the ingredients except the orange juice and orange slices in a big saucepan; stir to blend.
Bring to a boil, add in the orange juice, and return to a boil.
Decrease the heat to medium and simmer for 5 minutes.
**At this point, you can cool, strain, and refrigerate for up to 5 days.
Serve warm, garnished with orange slices.
To serve cold--fill a 12 ounce tumbler with ice; add 4 ounces cranberry punch, and fill the rest of the way with either sparkling water or 7-Up; garnish with orange slices.

name: Malibu Zinger
directions: Pour Malibu rum and peach schnapps into a hurricane glass three-quarters filled with ice. Slide down 2 drops of grenadine to the bottom. Add equal parts of pineapple juice and cranberry juice, leaving a little room at the top. Add a dollop of whipped cream, and drizzle grenadine down it. Ask the recipient to stir in the whipped cream before trying.

name: Spicy Margarita With Chili Salt
directions: In a small bowl combine the salt and 1 teaspoon chili powder.
Rub the rim of a glass with a lime wedge and dredge in the chili salt.
Fill a blender with the ice, tequila, lime juice, Grand Marnier, and superfine sugar.
Blenduntil smooth. Pour into the glasses. Garnish with lime wedge, if desired.

similar.name: Grapefruit Margaritas With Chipolte Chili Salt and Lime

name: Pomegranate Fizzies
directions: Fill wine flutes three-quarters full with sparkling wine. Add a generous splash of pomegranate juice to each, and drop in 1 teaspoons pomegranate seeds.

name: Low Fat Chocolate Egg Nog
ingredients: fat-free sugar-free instant chocolate pudding mix
directions: Combine cocoa mix with 1/4 cup hot water in a tall glass, and stir to dissolve. Add 1 cup soymilk and stir. Transfer to a blender.

Add remaining 4 cups soymilk and all other ingredients to the blender. Blend on high speed until smooth and uniform. Refrigerate in a covered container for at least a few hours to allow nog to thicken.
If you like, before serving, top each glass off with a squirt of Reddi-wip and a sprinkling of cinnamon. Enjoy!
MAKES 5 SERVINGS.

similar.name: No Guilt Hot Chocolate Mix in a Jar

name: Eggnog
directions: Beat eggs & vanilla until light & foamy.  Gradually beat in sugar & salt until thick.  Stir in cream & chill for a couple hours before serving.
Top with fresh ground nutmeg to serve.  Awesome!

name: Chai-Spiced Hot Cocoa
directions: Place cocoa mix into a cup. Gradually stir hot water into mix until blended. Add tea bag. Let stand 1 to 2 minutes, based on desired strength of chai spice. Remove tea bag and discard.
Top with a serving of Reddi-wip. Sprinkle with cinnamon, if desired.

name: Perfect Margarita
directions: Blend all ingredients in blender til smooth. Pour, serve, and enjoy!

name: Egg Nog
directions: Mix all ingredients until frothy.
Cover and chill.
Best if made a little in advance and refrigerated.

name: Homemade Wine
directions: Mix well and pour into gallon jug. Place a large round balloon (not inflated) on jar opening and set in cool semi-dark place. Balloon will gradually expand.
Wine will be ready for consumption in 3 weeks.

name: Instant Cappuccino Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together and pack in airtight jars.
To serve:  Place 4 tablespoons into a mug and add 1 cup of hot milk and stir.

name: Hot Cranberry Cider - Low Carb!!
directions: Put all ingredients in a pot, stir, and bring to a boil.  The longer it simmers, the more the spices come out.  You may need to add a little more water if it simmers long, as it gets stronger as the water evaporates.  You can also heat it in a crockpot.

name: Pumpkin Spice Coffee.
directions: This is listed by the cup. adjust quantities to your liking. I like alot of cream in my coffee. I mixed the spice and sugar together, and added it to my coffee with cream. I plan on mixing all together, and making a large batch to put in gift jars.

name: Cold Coffee Drink
directions: Put all in a blender, blend till smooth, and enjoy!

similar.name: Mr Coffee Slim Fast Mocha Frappe

name: Whiskey Sour Mix
directions: Mix well; put in plastic container and freeze at least 24 hours.  Spoon out into highball glasses.  Garnish with cherry and orange slice if desired.

name: Strawberry Daiquiri Mix
directions: Mix all together.  Put in plastic container and freeze 24 hours before serving.
Serve in a daiquiri glass and garnish with strawberry slices.

similar.name: Strawberry (Or Banana or Peach  or Pineapple) Frozen Daiquiris

name: Virgin Strawberry Margaritas
directions: Place ingredients in blender and pulse until crushed and slushy. Pour into serving glasses and garnish with whole strawberries and lime wedges.

name: Coconut Cloud Coffee
directions: In a saucepan, combine milk and coconut. Heat over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until mixture steams. Pour hot milk mixture into container of electric blender. Cover and blend on high speed until smooth. Strain.
In same saucepan, combine strained milk and hot coffee; heat thoroughly, but do not boil. Remove from heat; stir in rum extract.
Pour into 4 heat-proof glasses or coffee cups. Top each with a dollop of whipped cream. Sprinkle toasted coconut and toasted almonds over each serving. Serve immediately.

name: Mulled Wine
directions: Cook over medium heat until syrupy (about 30 minutes).  Add one gallon of burgundy wine and hold at serving temperature.  Float orange slices on top if desired. Serve in mugs.

TIP:  I watched Alton Brown make mulled wine recently on The Food Network.  He used a buffet sized coffee maker.  Just put all of the mulling ingredients in the ground coffee container.  Pour the water and wine into the reservoir and let it "perk" -- the pour spout makes it easy to serve.  My daughter says they generally have these large cofffe makers at the thrift shops.

name: 44 Cordial
directions: Poke 44 1 inch slits all over the orange with the tip of a paring knife.  Stuff a coffee bean into each slit.
Put the orange, sugar and rum in a wide mouthed jar with a tight sealing lid.  Place in a cool, dark spot, swirling the jar occasionally, for 44 days.
Remove and discard the orange.  Strain the liqueur through a cheesecloth lined strainer into a clean bottle and store in the refrigerator or freezer.  Serve neat or over ice.

name: Chocolate Cake Shot
directions: In shaker with ice, combine 1 part frangelico and 1 part vodka and shake up.
Coat lemon wedge in sugar and pour shot into glass.
Take the shot and follow with the lemon.
Have another! :).

name: Pomegranate Cocktail With Sparkling Wine
directions: Place zest in champagne flutes.
Add pomegranate juice and Cointreau.
Fill glasses with sparking wine or champagne.
Garnish with pomegranate seeds.

name: Maple Monkey Martini
directions: Fill cocktail shaker with ice.
Add both ingredients to shaker and shake well.
Pour into martini glass and serve.

name: Maple Chocolate Martini
directions: Fill cocktail with ice.
Add both ingredients and shake well.
Pour into martini glass and enjoy!

name: Cranberry Margaritas - the Lightship Restaurant
directions: Cranberry-Apple Puree:.

In a heavy saucepan, combine the brown sugar and 1/2 cup apple juice; bring the mixture to a boil, stirring, and add the cranberries.  Boil for 1 minute, stirring, reduce heat and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes or until the cranberries burst.
Let the mixture cool to room temperature.
In a food processor or blender, puree the cranberry mixture with the apples and the remaining 1/4 cup apple juice until it is smooth.  (Makes 1 1/2 cups; Cover and chill for up to 1 week).
Margaritas:.
In a blender, puree the ice with the teuila, triple sec, whiskey-sour mix, lime juice, and 1 1/2 cups of the cranberry-apple puree until the mixture is smooth.

name: Eggnog
directions: Separate the eggs. Mix the whites with vanilla and almond extract, and beat into stiff peaks. Mix the yolks with half the sugar. Mix the cream with remaining sugar, and beat into stiff peaks.
Carefully fold the yolks into the whites. Fold the cream into the egg mixture.
To make each serving, fold 2 ounces spirit into 1/10 of the mixture. Pour over a few ice cubes in a cocktail glass. Garnish with nutmeg and cinnamon.

name: Jamaican Sunset
directions: Stir together the sugar and rums in a 10oz mug.
Add the butter and fill the mug with boiling water.
Stir and sprinkle lightly with freshly grated nutmeg.
Enjoy!

name: Bloody Barbie
directions: Mix together,squeeze in fruits and enjoy.

name: Pumpkin Spice Latte
directions: Use a 2-quart slow cooker; add the coffee and milk to the stoneware.
Whisk in the pumpkin, spices, sugar, and vanilla.
Cover and cook on high for 2 hours; whisk again.
Ladle into mugs, and garnish with whipped cream and additional cinnamon; add in cinnamon stick and serve.

name: Homemade Baileys
directions: Mix well.
Refrigerate leftovers.
Yes there are uncooked eggs in this but I use fresh uncracked eggs and have never had a problem.
I used Jim Beam for the whiskey.

similar.name: Hot Bailey's Irish Cream Chocolate Drink

name: Blue Moon Special
directions: Fill a 6-8 oz glass with crushed ice.
Add vodka, Curacao & soda.
Give the lime a squeeze over the drink.
Garnish with a slice of lime & serve.

name: Swamp Tea
directions: combine, serve iced.

name: Mexican Hot Chocolate
directions: In large, heavy saucepan over medium-high heat, cook the milk until it is hot and almost boiling, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the cinnamon, vanilla and almond extracts and continue to heat for 1 minute.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof pitcher and serve. Garnish each serving with a cinnamon stick stirrer, if desired.

name: Iced Chocolate Cafe
directions: Place the chocolate milk ice cubes in a chilled glass. Add the coffee and chocolate milk.
Stir to combine.
Chocolate Milk Ice Cubes: Pour lowfat chocolate milk into ice cube trays and freeze.

name: Kahlua
directions: 1. Boil sugar and 3 cups water over medium heat until thick and syrupy. Remove from heat and cool slightly.
2. Combine boiling water, coffee and vanilla. Add to syrup.
3. After the mixture has cooled at least to room temperature add vodka. We store it in vodka bottles and store it in the fridge, although I have never heard of it going bad on the counter.
Note: When I double this recipe I use a half gallon of vodka and just enjoy the extra alcohol content.

name: Bahama Mama - Large Batch
directions: Combine all ingredients in large container.  Serve on ice.

name: Easy Cardamom Tea
directions: Add the boiling water to the teabag.
Drop in the mug the cardamon seed.
Serve with sugar or milk.

name: Click Frappuccino - Protein Shake
directions: Put it in a blender and whiz it.
Top with whipped cream -- sip -- die -- go to heaven -- .

name: Bennigan's Paddy O’ Punch
directions: Pour contents in a huge, fishbowl-like glass over lots of ice. Garnish with pineapple slices, orange slices and cherries. Make sure you use the cool swords.

name: Sugar-Free Egg Nog
directions: Place all ingredients in the blender and blend away!
Serve chilled in glasses with a sprinkle of nutmeg on top.

similar.name: No Guilt Hot Chocolate Mix in a Jar

name: Classic Sangria
directions: In a heatproof pitcher,  place wine, juice and fruit slices.  In a small saucepan, combine sugar, water, brandy and Cointreau and bring to a simmer, stirring until sugar is dissolved.
Add to wine mixture.  Refrigerate, covered, for at least one hour.
Pour sangria into 8 white wine glasses or old-fashioned glassed filled with ice.

name: Fuzzy Nuts
directions: Mix and serve on the rocks.

name: Coquito (Puerto Rican Eggnog)
directions: Add the cloves, cinnamon sticks, and the ginger to the water and bring to a boil. Remove water from heat and let sit for 15 minutes to cool down. Remove all ingredients from the water and pour water into a blender. Add the Coco Lopez, evaporated milk and the rum into the blender. Blend all ingredients for about 30 seconds. Pour in bottles and refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving.
Tip: You can add a cinnamon stick to each of the bottles to give it a better flavor.

name: 5-Spice Tea
directions: Bring water to a boil in saucepan.
Add tea leaves and simmer for 10 minutes.
Place milk in microwavable container and microwave until steaming.
Add milk to tea mixture, and stir in 5-spice powder.  Simmer 1 minute.
Strain tea into mugs, or refrigerate for iced tea.  (strain into glasses over ice).
Sweeten to taste, with sweetener of choice.

name: Vanilla Latte
directions: Slice vanilla bean and scrape out the seeds, using a sharp pointed knife.
Combine vanilla bean, seeds and milk in a pot and let soak for an hour.
After soaking one hour, slowly bring to a boil, remove the vanilla bean and foam the milk.
Divide steamed milk into two tall glasses, then slowly pour half of the coffee into each glass (not in the middle but from the rim down the side of the glass).
If you want sweet coffee, you can sweeten the coffee before pouring into the milk.
Sprinkle with cocoa powder and enjoy!

similar.name: Matcha Oat Milk Latte (Hot or Iced)

name: Sweet Egg Cappuccino
directions: Beat egg and sugar until foamy.
Slowly beat in hot coffee - carefully or the egg will curdle!
Serve immediately.
Enjoy!

name: Strawberry Blonde Martini (The Melting Pot)
directions: Combine the vodka, daiquiri mix and pinapple juice.
Float the Champagne on the top.
Garnish with strawberry.
Enjoy!

name: Pepper's Spicy Cocoa
directions: Mix all ingredients in a large bowl.
Scoop into a container.
Direction to make hot chocolate: Place 2 Tablespoons for every 8 oz of hot milk.

similar.name: Cinnamon Coffee Cocoa Mix in a Jar

name: Lgm Margarita
directions: Place all ingredients in a shaker filled with ice. (Lasco Sweet & Sour Lemon is best).
Shake until frothy.
Pour into salt-rimmed glass.
Sit down on the couch.

name: Nougat Hot Chocolate With Whipped Cream - Rachael Ray
directions: In a small saucepan, heat 1 1/2 cups milk, 1/2 cup heavy cream and 1/2 cup toasted slivered almonds over medium heat. Steep, covered, for 10 minutes.
In a bowl, whip 1/4 cup heavy cream until soft peaks form; fold in 1/2 tablespoon toasted slivered almonds.
Strain the milk, discarding the almonds, then return to the saucepan. Heat over medium heat; whisk in 3 tablespoons honey, 1 ounce chopped chocolate and 1 pinch salt.
Pour into 2 mugs; top with the whipped cream and some chopped almonds.

similar.name: Happiness in a Cup Hot Chocolate

name: Eggnog With Fluffy Topping
directions: Mix egg yolks and sugar until thorouhghly blended.
Slowly add milk and cream, mixing thoroughly.
Put in fridge.
Blend egg whitles until stif peaks form.
To serve, ladel nog into a cup, spoon some fowm ontop, and sprinkle with nutmed.
Note: you can add sugar to egg white.

name: Ready Mint Juleps
directions: Remove 1/3 cup of bourbon from the bottle.
In its place, put a cup of crushed and twisted fresh mint leaves.
Let this steep for 3-5 months.
To serve, put a bit of sugar - to taste - in a cup with lots of shaved ice and pour the bourbon over it.
Can garnish with a sprig of fresh mint.
Most authentic when served in a silver cup with a glass straw.

similar.name: Mint Syrup for Mint Juleps or Mojitos

name: Double Berry Martini
directions: Empty drink mix into large pitcher.
Add water, cranberry juice and vodka;stir until drink mix is completely dissolved. Refrigerate at least 1 hour or until chilled.
Stir in club soda just before serving. Spoon cranberries evenly into 6 martini glasses; top with the beverage mixture.

name: Raspberry Cosmo
directions: In a cocktail shaker, add the vodka, Grand Marnier, lime juice, and cranberry juice.
Add ice; cover and shake until very cold; strain into a chilled martini glass.

name: Moulin Rouge
directions: In a cocktail shaker, combine the rum, pomegranate juice, and Grand Marnier.
Add ice; cover and shake until very cold.
Strain into a chilled glass; garnish with a skewer of orange wedge and raspberries.

name: Sandra Lee's Rose Sangria
directions: Combine wine, Sprite®, rum, and brandy in a clear glass pitcher filled halfway with ice cubes.
Stir with a long wooden spoon.
Add a few slices of apple, halved grapes, and lemon and lime slices to pitcher.
Stir once more.
Pour sangría into tulip glasses.

name: The Recipe (The Waltons)
directions: Mix first 4 ingredients together in the Tupperware Fix & Mix bowl.
Add the Sprite and 7-up. Mix thoroughly.
Place lid on top and freeze for 2 or 3 days.(Stir every once and a while).
The morning you want to use it, take out of the freezer to thaw slightly.
Drink should be icy.
It can be re-froze.

similar.name: Concentrate for Refreshing Tea, Iced or Hot

name: Simple Syrup - Cranberry Orange for Champagne Cocktail
directions: Place all ingredients EXCEPT ice cubes in a  medium sauce pan.
Bring to a boil, turn down the heat  to med-high.
Cook for 2 minutes or until sugar is dissolved.
Remove from the heat and pour through a wire mesh strainer, pressing berries with the back of a spoon to release their juices.
Discard solids.
Stir in 1/2 C ice cubes to cool.
Freeze for 20 minutes to further cool.
Add 1 - 2 Tablespoons to your favorite sparkling wine. I love Prosecco.
Float a few frozen cranberries in the glass and watch them dance in the bubbles!

name: Non-Alcoholic Shirley Temple
directions: Pour first 2 ingredeints over ice in a collins glass and stir.  Add ginger ale and seltzer, then garnish with cherry.

similar.name: Out of This World - Mai Tai

name: New Orleans Day Drink
directions: Place all ingredients in a blender and blend on high speed until smooth.  Pour into an old-fashion glasas.

name: Midori Glow
directions: Fill a short glass with ice.
Pour in vodka and melon liqueur, and stir.
Top off with lemon lime soda, and serve.

similar.name: Midnight Potion (Aka Pure H. E . Double Hocky Sticks)

name: Flirtini
directions: In a cocktail shaker, combine vodka, pineapple juice, cranberry juice, rum, and sweet-and-sour mix.
Add ice; cover and shake until very cold.
Strain into a chilled martini glass.
Garnish with coconut pieces and cranberries.

name: Paradise Martini
directions: Fill a cocktail shaker with ice.
Add vodka, cold water, creme de cassis, and amaretto.
Squeeze in lime juice; cover and shake vigorously.
Strain into chilled martini glass.
Garnish with cherry.

name: David's Sweet Iced Tea
directions: Tie tea bags together.
Bring 4 cups water to boil, add tea bags and take off heat.
Cover and let sit for about an hour.
Pour tea into gallon container and squeeze tea bag excess into gallon container with clean hands.
Add sugar and stir.
Finish filling container with water, stir and serve.

name: Orange Slushie
directions: Add ice and orange juice into a blender.
Blend on medium/high until ice is chunked.
Pour into cups and serve.

name: Better Than Mc Donald's Sweet Tea
directions: Place tea bags in a 4 Cup glass measuring cup.
Add baking soda.
Fill with water past 4 Cup line to about 4 1/4 cups.
Cover thightly with aluminum foil for 15 minutes.
Remove / drain tea bags.
Add sugar to a seperate large picture.
Add hot tea concentrate to picture.
Stir until sugar is completely melted.
Add ice to picture.
Serve immediately or store in refridgerator.

similar.name: Perfect Sweet Tea (Secret of the South)

name: Spiced Orange Mulled Mead (Adult)
directions: Put all ingredients in a medium sacepan with a tight-fitting lid. Heat and bring to a simmer; reduce and don't allow to boil. Let warm with the lid on for 10-20 minutes to allow the flavours to combine. Serve warm. .

name: Dolce and Gabbana
directions: Fill an 8-oz Old Fashioned glass with a good amount of ice.
Add Diet Coke, and then grenadine (or vice versa).
Stir together with a stirring stick.
Garnish with an orange wheel.

name: Butterscotch Chocolate Milk (Adults)
directions: Mix and enjoy!

name: Irish Root Beer
directions: Mix & enjoy!

name: Chilli Cordial
directions: Add water to a saucepan and simmer chillies for 15-20mins.
Strain and put water back into saucepan.
Add sugar and boil for 5 minutes.
Add remaining ingredients and bring to the boil.
Bottle and keep in a cool place.
Throw out if it looks or smells odd.

name: White Chocolate Chai Latte
