Strain into crushed ice-filled highball glasses. Garnish with the berry halves and mint sprigs.

name: Moby Duck
directions: pour ginger ale into a cup.
add the rum.
splash in the coffemate irish cream.
wait for it to bubble.
enjoy!

name: Cocojito
directions: Blend the ice, limes, cream of coconut, rum, and mint leaves together in a blender until smooth. Pour into two glasses and garnish each with a mint sprig and lime wedge as desired.

name: Kentucky Colonel
directions: Fill a highball glass with ice cubes.
Add the bourbon, Southern Comfort, triple sec, orange juice, juice of 1/2 lemon and simple syrup to a shaker; shake the mixture vigorously for 5-10 seconds.
Pour the contents of the shaker into the glass and garnish with a cherry.

name: Grilled Citrus Spritzer
directions: Preheat barbecue and lightly oil grill.
Slice one lemon and one orange into 1/2-inch thick slices.  Divide the citrus slices between the 6 skewers.  Cut remaining citrus fruits in half.
Grill citrus slices untl caramelized and marked on both sides, about 2 minutes per side; remove.  Grill citrus halves cut side down until well-caramelized and warm 5-8 minutes.

Squeeze and strain the juice from the grilled halves into a pitcher.  Stir in honey and a pinch of salt until dissolved.  Add cranberry cocktail, wine, carbonated water and ice.  To serve put a citrus swizzle stick and a mint sprig in each glass.

name: The Jillian Special
directions: Mix 2 ounces vodka, 1 ounce Cointreau and 8 ounces of grapefruit juice (no sugar added).  Serve over ice and top it off with a lime slice if you like.

name: Russian Standard Moscow Mule
directions: Pour two ounces of Russian Standard Vodka over ice in a chilled Russian Standard copper mug or Collins glass.  Add 5 ounces of ginger beer and stir gently.  Garnish with a squeezed wedge of lime.   For additional recipes, please visit www.RussianStandardMoscowMule.com.

name: Bourbon Smash
directions: Add raspberries and mint leaves to a highball glass; muddle the ingredients in the glass.
Fill the glass with crushed ice.
Add in the bourbon, cranberry juice, juice from 1/2 lime, and simple syrup.
Garnish with a sprig of mint and stir to drink.

name: Pineapple Beer
directions: Discard pineapple stalk.
Wash unskinned pineapple well and chop up with skin and all.
Place all ingredients into a large container with a lid and stir to mix well.  Cover and leave where it can be undisturbed.

The fermentation will start immediately.  Stir twice a day over the next 72 hours.  Don't worry - it is supposed to smell like fermented fruit!  By the third day this smell will subside to a lovely beery, cidery smell.
Strain through a sieve or cheesecloth and bottle.

similar.name: Jackie's Marvelous and Easy Pineapple Punch

name: Red Fox
directions: Pour cranberry juice in the bottom of the glass.  Add iced tea.  Splash lemonade on the top.  Do not mix!  Enjoy.

name: Spiced Coffee Latte
directions: Heat coffee and spices in medium saucepan until almost boiling.
Reduce to low and simmer 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and strain through sieve to remove spices.
Whisk in Eagle Brand Sweetened Condensed Milk.  Serve immediately.

name: Rooibos Vanilla Tea Latte
directions: Combine boiling water and tea bags in medium bowl.  Let steep 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, place 1 1/2 tablespoons Eagle Brand in each of 4 serving glasses.  Top with tea, and serve.
Let guests stir to combine - Enjoy!

name: Chocolate Peppermint Swizzle
directions: Stir together Eagle Brand and cocoa powder.
Simmer, stirring constantly until heated through.  Remove from heat.
Stir in vanilla or peppermint extract.  Serve warm in mugs with a peppermint swizzle stick or candy cane; top with whipped cream.

name: Pumpkin Pie Martini
directions: Combine ingredients, shake and strain into a graham cracker-rimmed martini shell.  Top with pumpkin whipped cream.  Garnish with a dash of allspice.

name: Almond Toffee Martini
directions: Fill a shaker halfway full of ice. Pour in almond milk, crème de cacao, butterscotch liqueur and vodka. Cover and shake well.  Strain into a martini glass and serve.

Note : For a delicious garnish, rim the glass in cocoa powder or sugar crystals/ crystallized sugar. First, pour a teaspoon or 2 of almond milk into a saucer. Then, place a teaspoon or 2 of cocoa powder in a saucer. Dip the rim first in almond milk, then one or two times in cocoa powder. Reserve glass until ready to fill.

name: Watermelon Mojitos
directions: Use a mortar and pestle to smash mint leaves.  Remove the rind and seeds from watermelon.  Put fruit in a food processor fitted with the steel blade and puree.  Put the mashed mint in a large pitcher with 2 cups of the watermelon puree, the rum, simple syrup, and lime juice.  Stir to combine.
Put ice cubes in glasses, pour mojito mixture over, and garnish with mint.

name: Pumpkin Spice White Hot Chocolate
directions: In a heavy saucepan, combine 2 cups of milk, white chocolate, cocoa powder and sugar.
Cook over medium heat, whisking periodically, until the chocolate is melted and the mixture is well combined.
Whisk in the pumpkin puree, cinnamon and ground pumpkin pie spices.
Add the remaining milk, 1 cup at a time, until the desired consistency is reached, (adding more if necessary).
Serve topped with low-fat whipped cream and a sprinkling of ground cinnamon, if like.
NOTE: just be sure not to fill the mugs too full before adding the whipped cream, or you might have some overflow.

name: Madison Cocktail - Pear Jelly Bean Explosion
directions: Shake and serve over few cubes of ice. I tried Absolut and Smirnoff vodkas and both work wonderfully. Frangelico is a must! Tried this with regular lemonade and it's just not the same.
ENJOY!

name: Spiced Ginger Coffee
directions: In a small bowl, combine the molasses, brown sugar, ginger, and cinnamon.
For each serving, add 2 teaspoons molasses mixture in a mug; add 1 cup hot coffee; stir until combined.
Serve with milk, whipped cream, and cinnamon, if desired.
Cover and store remaining molasses mixture in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

name: Texas Hot Bloody Mary
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Store in a sealed container. I poured it back into the V8 container and pulled the label off.
Mix with vodka to your taste and serve over ice.
Garnish with celery, limes, olives or other pickled cocktail veggies.

name: Luxurious Hot Chocolate
directions: Combine all the ingredients in the insert of a 5-7 quart slow cooker.
Cover and cook on LOW for 1 hour.
Stir up the chocolate from the bottom of the pan with a whisk.
Cover and cook on LOW for another 3 hours.
Serve hot chocolate in the cooker set on warm.

similar.name: Easy and Delicious Real Hot Chocolate

name: Fluffernutter Hot Cocoa
directions: In a small saucepan over medium, heat the milk until hot but not boiling. Whisk in the Fluff, chocolate chips and peanut butter chips. Whisk until completely melted and smooth. To serve, top with additional Fluff.

name: Fúkt Öp the Swedish Multi Hammer
directions: Fill a pint glass with ice.
Pour in the vodka and the juice.
Add Lemon Line soda.
Stir to combine.
Get fúkt and build a desk.

similar.name: Magic Bullet a Bullet to the Head

name: Italian Stallion (Bloody Mary Italia)
directions: For 6 drinks:
Pour vodka, tomato juice, worchestershire, and pesto into a large pitcher.
Stir in chili powder, cumin, and pepper.
Cut zucchini in thin strips or using a decorative mini-cookie cutter, make fun shapes.
Using a slice of lemon, wet the rim of each glass.
Dip the glass into salt, making sure the rim has salt all around.
Add ice to glasses.
Pour mixture into each glass, add olives and thin zucchini spear for stirring  or use zucchini shapes and olives on a toothpick. Set on the edge of each glass.
Add a straw.
For one drink:
1 oz vodka.
5 oz tomato.
Dash of each spice.
1/2 t pesto and worchestershire.
Mix in glass and serve.

name: Cherry Fun Dip Shooter
directions: Combine all in a shaker with ice.
Shake well, strain and serve.

name: Dad's Egg Nog
directions: Mix all ingredients in a blender 1-2 minutes on high.
Enjoy!

similar.name: Aunt Emma's Old Fashioned Egg Nog

name: Brazilian Rum Coffee
directions: In a large saucepan combine cocoa and salt.  Stir in sweetened condensed milk.
Over medium heat, slowly stir in water and coffee; heat thoroughly but do not boil.  Stir in rum extract.  Serve immediately.
Garnish with whipped cream for a nice treat!

name: Yule Mule Cocktail
directions: Juice 6 limes. Cut remaining 3 limes into small wedges. Pour vodka and 1 cup lime juice into a carafe or jug. Stir to combine.
Half-fill twelve 300ml (10 oz) capacity  glasses with crushed ice. Pour over 1/3 cup vodka mixture. Top with ginger beer and lime wedges. Serve.
Note- You can sub ginger ale for the ginger beer, but it won't have as much kick - try Vernors if you do.

name: Hungry Girl No-Nonsense Nog
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender, and blend on high speed until mixed thoroughly. Refrigerate in a covered container for at least a few hours to allow nog to thicken.
If you like, before serving, top each glass off with a squirt of Reddi-wip and a sprinkling of cinnamon. Yum time!

similar.name: No Guilt Hot Chocolate Mix in a Jar
Aunt Emma's Old Fashioned Egg Nog

name: Homemade Kahlua
directions: Combine sugar and 2 cups of water.  Bring to a boil and boil for 5 minutes.  Gradually stir in coffee using a wire whisk.  Cool.  Pour into a tall bottle.  Add vodka and vanilla.  Let stand for two weeks.

name: Sweet Tea and Limoncello Martinis
directions: Combine the first 3 ingredients in a large pitcher.
Cover and chill thoroughly.
Serve in chilled martini glasses; garnish, if desired.
For each serving--fill a shaker half full of crushed ice; add 3/4 cup tea mixture.
Cover and shake until thoroughly chilled; strain into a chilled martini glass; garnish if desired and serve immediately.

name: Bubbling Rubies
directions: Place a spoonful of pomegranate seeds in a chilled martini glass.
In a cocktail shaker, combine the white cranberry juice, vodka, peach liqueur, and lime juice with ice.
Shake vigorously, and strain into a chilled martini glass; top with champagne and serve.

name: Junipear Freeze
directions: Do ahead: In a blender, purée entire can of pears. Pour purée into separate bowl/pitcher and set aside. Rinse blender bowl and return to base. To make two cocktail drinks: Pour juice of 2 oranges into blender. Add 2/3 cup pear purée, gin and sugar.

Fill a 14-ounce glass with ice.  Pour ice into blender and repeat, adding another glass of ice. Blend until smooth. Pour into cocktail glasses. Garnish with orange wheels. Serving glass: 14-ounce cocktail glass (daiquiri/poco grande).
Nutritional Information Per Serving:
Calories per drink: 280 (based on 80-proof gin)**
*Note: One 15-ounce can of pears in heavy syrup makes 6 drinks.
**Note: Higher-proof gin contributes more calories.

similar.name: Strawberry (Or Banana or Peach  or Pineapple) Frozen Daiquiris

name: Old Virginia Wassail Cider
directions: In a large stock pot over medium heat, combine the apple cider, orange juice, pineapple juice, cinnamon sticks, cloves and honey. Bring to a boil, then simmer over low heat, or transfer to a slow cooker to keep warm while serving. Strain out cinnamon sticks and cloves before serving if desired.

name: Mandarin Martini
directions: put all ingredients into a shaker full of ice --
shake shake shake -- pour into a cold martini glass.
enjoy!

name: Low Calorie Martini
directions: Combine ingredients in a martini shaker.  Shake.  Shake a little more.  Then shake it.  Pour into chilled martini glasses.  If you want to get fancy then garnish with some fruit that compliments your flavors of powder and vodka.

name: Spiced Rum Breakfast Coffee
directions: In basket of drip coffee make place coffee grounds, cinnamon stick, and the 3 cardamom seed pods broken open.
Add 10 cups of water and brew.
When done brewing add 1 teaspoon Rum extract to brewed coffee.
Serve. Can add milk and sweetener if you like.

name: Traditional Coquito (With Eggs/ Cooked)
directions: In a 2 quart saucepan, heat water and cinnamon sticks to boiling over high heat. Reducei heat to medium and cook until liquid is reduced to one cup. Remove cinnamon sticks and set liquid aside to cool to room temperature.        In a 3 quart.Saucepan with a wire whisk, beat egg yolks and evaporated milk until well-mixed.
Cook over low heat, stirring constantly until mixture thickens and coats a spoon - about 10 minutes (do not boil) set aside to cool slightly.
When cinnamon flavored liquid has cooled stir in coconut milk, until well mixed.
. In serving bowl, combine coconut mixture, yolk mixture, sweetened condensed milk and rum. Chill well and serve.

similar.name: Hot Buttered Rum W/ Brandy, Batter

name: Gatsby Cocktail
directions: Pour all ingredients into a shaker glass with ice.  Shake hard. Strain into a martini glass and enjoy!

name: Sweet Vermouth Floats
directions: Pour vermouth into 4 short glasses.
Dollop in sorbet using a melon ball scoop. Garnish with orange twists.

name: Blood Orange Sunrises
directions: Pour 2 tablespoons nectar into each of 4 fluted glasses.
Holding a tablespoon right side up over nectar, pour in juice, allowing it to trickle from the bowl of the spoon into the glass (this will keep the colors separate).

name: Seabreeze Punch
directions: Combine ingredients in a punchbowl or pitcher. Stir to mix.

name: Sangria
directions: Mix ingredients in a large pitcher.

name: Cosmopolitan...on the Sweeter Side
directions: Mix all ingredients and put in a shaker with ice. If you are serving a group, mix in a pitcher and pour servings in the shaker. Shake and pour into martini glasses and enjoy!

name: Peppy Tomato Sipper
directions: In a small pitcher stir together tomato juice, lime juice, Worcestershire sauce, horseradish and  hot sauce.
Serve over ice.

name: Orange Ginger Margarita
directions: Place frozen orange juice concentrate (reserve 1 tablespoon), tequila, ginger liqueur, ginger, and ice in a blender and mix until ice is crushed.

Place reserved tablespoon concentrate on a small plate and salt on another small plate. Dip rims of 4 glasses in concentrate and then in salt. Fill glasses, garnish with crystallized ginger if desired, and serve.

name: Cosmo Cubana
directions: Muddle lime slices and mint leaves in a cocktail shaker or pint glass.
Add all other ingredients and plenty of ice.
Shake and then strain into martini glasses.
garnish with fresh mint leaves and enjoy!

name: Beautiful Blue Vodka Martini
directions: Place ice into the martini shaker. About 3/4's full.
Add first 3 liquid ingredients to martini shaker using a one ounce jigger.
Put the lid on the shaker, grab a hand towel and grab the shaker and shake like crazy for at least 30 seconds.
Using the strainer pour into your martini glasses and add one drop of Grenadine if you wish.
Garnish as you wish with an orange slice, lemon slice or olives or whatever you prefer. I like lemon.
Have fun and drink safe!

name: Hurricane
directions: Pour all ingredients over ice in a tall hurricane glass. Enjoy!

name: Homemade Bailey's Irish Cream
directions: Blend for one minute.  Keep refrigerated.  Makes almost one fifth.

name: Copycat Jamaican Cowboy Margarita
directions: Combine and pour over crushed ice in a margarita glass or brandy snifter.

name: Strawberry Margarita Using Frozen Strawberries in Syrup
directions: Place it all in blender and blend until smooth.

name: Hurricane
directions: Stir all ingredients with ice in a tall hurricane glass.

name: Quick Nausea Remedy
directions: Boil all ingredients (except for the 7up) together for 10-15 minutes so the oils from the ginger escape into your drink.
Pour your drink through a strainer into your cup, leaving room for a bit of 7up.
Add 7up (diet or regular) to your drink to taste and drink slowly!

similar.name: I Am Sick As a Dog Tea

name: Lloyd's Hot Buttered Rum Batter
directions: In a food processor or heavy duty mixer, cream together butter and sugar  until smooth. Add in cinnamon, nutmeg, and allspice. Spices may be adjusted to taste; if you prefer more cinnamon, add it! Mix into ice cream, and beat until smooth.
May be stored in refrigerator or freezer until use.

To serve, place 1-2 Tablespoons of mixture in the bottom of a mug. fill 3/4 full with hot water. Stir until dissolved. Add Rum, or white Christmas to taste. Add a pinch of additional nutmeg to the top.

similar.name: Hot Buttered Rum W/ Brandy, Batter
Awesome and Economical Hot Buttered Rum Mix

name: Boomette's Apricot White Sangria for 1
directions: Add everything in a wine glass or sangria glass.  For me it was perfect but for DH it was too sweet.  So you may need to adjust to suit your taste.

name: Faolans Spiced Cranberry Orange Mead
directions: Amounts listed in recipe are for each gallon of product desired (except for yeast, 1 packet will do up to five gallons of mix).
Heat water to 160 degrees and slowly stir in honey, let cook for 10 minutes then stir in fruit juices.
In fermentation container (carbouy) place spices.
Let honey mixture cool to room temperature then pour into carbouy, add yeast, and install (place) fermentation lock.
Place in cool, dark place for about three months or until fermentation stops, then bottle in clean and sterlized bottles.

name: Sugar Free Supreme Cocoa Mix
directions: Place all ingredients in food processor and pulse till well blended and a fine powder.
Store in air tight container or ziplock bag.
Add 1/2 cup of mix to 8 oz. hot water. Stirring well to dissolve.
Add more or less mix depending on personal tastes.
Can top with Cool Whip sugar free topping.
Enjoy and thanks again PAchefRebecca.

similar.name: Fat Free Sugar Free Hot Chocolate Mix

name: Cucumberade
directions: Thinly slice one cucumber and reserve.
Peel, seed, and coarsely chop the second cucumber.
Puree the chopped cucumber with the lemon and lime juices, salt, and 1 cup water in a blender.
Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a large pitcher, using a ladle or rubber spatula to extract as much juice from the pulp as possible.
Add the remaining water and the cucumber slices; stir gently.
Refrigerate a couple of hours before serving.

name: The Seductive Swan
directions: Muddle four blackberries in bottom of a tumbler.
Add ice, Russian Standard Vodka and lemonade.
Garnish with remaining blackberry.

name: Milk-Chocolate and Peanut Butter Hot Cocoa
directions: Warm milk and cream in a medium saucepan over a medium-low heat.  Add chocolate, and whisk until melted and combined.  Continue to warm mixture until thick, about 5 minutes.  Whisk in peanut butter until smooth.

name: Slow Cooker Spiced Cranberry-Apple Cider
directions: Add all ingredients to a 4- to 5-quart slow cooker; mix well.
Cover; cook on LOW for 4-6 hours.
Before serving, may remove allspice and cinnamon.

name: Bonefish Winter White Cosmo
directions: 1. Mix juice and liquor together in a shaker with ice.
2. Shake well.
3. Pour into martini glass.
4. Add cranberries as garnish.

name: Chile's Presidente Margarita With a Twist
directions: Combine all ingredients in a metal shaker/sifter. Add 5 ice cubes and shake until shaker is cold to touch. Sift into a salted rim glass of ice over a cheesecloth or a small screen, I use a reusable coffee filter, to catch the mint leaves and enjoy! For frozen simply add  to blender with 1 1/2 cups of crushed ice and blend.

name: Squirt of Citrus
directions: Cut all the fruit in half and squeeze the juices into a 2-cup measuring cup.
Pour all the juice through a fine-mesh strainer into a large pitcher.
Add the water, stir, and serve over ice.

name: Havana Special
directions: Shake with ice and strain into a cocktail glass. Repeat.

name: Happy Honey Annie
directions: Shake well with ice and strain into a cocktail glass.

name: Hannibal Hamlin Cocktail
directions: Shake with ice and strain into a cocktail glass.

name: Deauville Cocktail
directions: Stir with ice and strain into a cocktail glass.

name: Dirty Mother
directions: Stir with ice and strain into an ice-filled Old Fashioned glass. Top with milk.

name: Organic Cherry Martini
directions: 1. Chill your martini glass in a freezer for about 5 minutes. Alternatively, fill the glass with ice and water for faster chilling.
2. Add cherry juice, grapefruit juice, agave and vodka into a cocktail shaker filled with ice and shake moderately (or vigorously if you like your drink foamy).
3. Strain into a chilled glass.
4. Garnish with a lemon/lime/grapefruit twist and/or cherry and enjoy!

name: Champagne Cocktail
directions: In a champagne flute, stir together the bitters, lemon juice and maple syrup; once combined, pour in the champagne.
Squeeze the lemon wedge into the flute, then wipe the rim with it and discard.

similar.name: La Vie En Rose Champagne Cocktail

name: Keeler Tavern's Mulled Cider
directions: Pour gallon of cider in a very large pot.  Put spices in a cheesecloth and add to cider.  Bring to a boil.
Simmer uncovered for about 30 minutes.
Let stand for 15 minutes with heat off.  Remove spice bag.
Chill until needed, then reheat.
Serve warm.

name: Carob Spice Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.
Also Note: When you are buying carob chips, make sure you buy roasted ones, or it won't taste right.

name: Peppermint Fennel Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Chamomile Mint Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Spiced Chamomile Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

similar.name: Green Tea With Indian Chai Spices

name: Chamomile Lavender Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Hibiscus Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.
Chill in fridge and drink as an iced tea!

name: Vanilla Red Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Make sure you chop the vanilla bean into little pieces first.  Note: Food.com made me list the rooibos under "rooibos tea bags", but I used loose-leaf red rooibos tea.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Vanilla Chai Tea
directions: Put water and spices (except for the black tea) into a small pot.
Add water and cook on medium for 6 minutes, then add black tea.
Place lid on pot and cook for 6 minutes.
Add in milk and stir, then turn off the stove burner.
Add sugar and stir until completely dissolved.
Strain spices out and enjoy!

name: Red Vanilla Chai Tea
directions: Put water and spices (except for the rooibos tea) into a small pot.
Add water and cook on medium for 6 minutes, then add rooibos tea.
Place lid on pot and cook for 6 minutes.
Add in milk and stir, then turn off the stove burner.
Add sugar and stir until completely dissolved.
Strain spices out and enjoy!

name: Flowery Black Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Spiced Rose Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Jasmine Rose Black Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Mint Rose Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.
Note: If you don't have peppermint and spearmint on hand, you can substitute any type of mint, or just use one type if that's all you have.

name: Lemon Vanilla White Tea
directions: Cut the vanilla bean into little pieces first.
Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Sarsaparila Spice Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

similar.name: Green Tea With Indian Chai Spices

name: Lavender Mint Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Mint Tarragon Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: All - Rose Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.
Note: If you don't have two different colors of rose petals, then just use 2 parts rose petals (of a single color) and 1 part rosehips.

name: White Rose Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Lavender White Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.
This tea is best chilled.

name: Rooibos Anise Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.  Note that food.com has forced me to list the rooibos as "rooibos tea bags" but I used loose leaf tea.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Rooibos Carob Tea
directions: Cut the vanilla bean into little pieces.

Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.  Note that food.com forced me to list the rooibos as "rooibos tea bags" but I used loose leaf tea.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Rooibos Orange Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.  Note that I was forced by food.com to list the rooibos as "rooibos tea bags" but I actually used loose leaf tea.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Rosemary Apple Pie Tea
directions: Cut the vanilla bean into little pieces.
Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Chamomile Apple Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Spiced Anise Tea
directions: Cut the vanilla bean into small pieces first.
Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

similar.name: Green Tea With Indian Chai Spices

name: Rosemary Lavender Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Caramel Apple Cider
directions: Stir together the brown sugar and  whipping cream in a saucepan.
Cook, stirring constantly, over medium heat 2 minutes or until bubbly.
Stir in vanilla and apple cider.
Cook 10 minutes or until thoroughly heated, stirring often.
Garnish with whipped cream, a drizzle of warmed caramel sauce, and a sprinkle of ground cinnamon.

similar.name: Spiced Apple Cider With Rum Whipped Cream

name: Bine
directions: Pour wine into a 16-ounce glass.
Add beer.

name: Vodvilla
directions: Mix and stir. Serve over ice.

name: St. Germain Gin and Tonic
directions: Combine all ingredients in a tall ice-filled Collins glass and stir well.
Garnish with a orange wedge (I actually give it a squeeze in the glass as well).

name: Pineapple-Rum Jello Shots
directions: Add boiling water to jug or bowl with pouring lip. I would recommend pouring some extra boiling water in first to warm up the jug.
Add jello and stir well for at least 2 minutes.
Add cold water and then rum.
Pour into shot glasses but be careful not to overfill as it is hard to eat them if they are much more than a mouthful.
Refrigerate for at least two hours.
If you use small glasses rather than plastic or paper cups, serve with toothpicks so that it is easier to pull out the jello.
Feel free to use any rum you like, even cheaper brands. Good luck.

name: Sangria Francais
directions: Add all the ingredients except the 7-Up in a large glass or ceramic container; stir well.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours.
Serve over ice; top with 7-Up, if desired and garnish with additional strawberries.

name: Irish Vanilla Blues
directions: Pour the whiskey into a glass.
Add the vanilla and blueberries.
Desecrate it with ice if you must.
Enjoy!

name: Carob Mint Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Cinnamon Vanilla Carob Tea
directions: Cut the vanilla bean into little pieces.
Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Carob Rose Tea
directions: Assemble all the ingredients and place in a tea ball.  Note: If you don't own a tea ball, then you can put the ingredients directly in the water and then strain out later.
Place tea ball in hot water and steep for 10 minutes.

name: Blueberry Pomegranate Sangria
directions: In a saucepan, combine the blueberries with the simple syrup over low heat.
Cook, stirring constantly, until the blueberries start to discolor and the syrup starts to thicken, about 5 minutes; set aside.
Combine the remaining ingredients in a large ceramic or glass container.
Add the blueberry mixture (liquid and berries); stir well.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours; serve over ice.

name: Pink Peppercorn Thyme Soda
directions: Toast anise, peppercorns and cloves in a small skillet over medium-high heat until aromatic, stirring often, about 2 minutes.
Wrap spices in triple layer of cheesecloth; tie shut.
Pound with a rolling pin to crush spices lightly.
Bring 3 cups water and 1 cup sugar to boil in medium saucepan. Add spices in cheesecloth. Remove from heat; cover and allow to steep 15 minutes.
Add 1/2 cup thyme sprigs. Cover and steep 10 minutes longer.
Strain syrup into medium bowl. Chill until cold.
To serve: Fill 8 tall glasses with ice. Divide syrup among glasses. Add 1/2 cup soda to each glass. Garnish with thyme sprigs and peppercorns.

name: Pomegranate Gin Sling
directions: Combine first 4 ingredients; divide evenly between 2 chilled glasses; divide seeds between glasses. Garnish with lime slices.

name: Vanilla Frappuccino Blended Coffee
directions: Place all of the ingredients in blender, except for the Reddi-wip. Blend on high speed until well blended.
Pour into a tall glass, and finish off with whipped topping.

name: Tequila Sunset
directions: Fill a collins glass with ice cubes.  Add tequila and orange juice, and stir well.
Top with blackberry brandy, and let the brandy slowly settle and fall through the orange juice layer.
Add a cherry on top, and serve.

name: The Russian Sundae
directions: Pour vodka and coffee liqueur over ice cubes in an old-fashioned glass. Fill with chocolate milk and mix. Garnish with the maraschino cherry.

name: Mango Tango Tea
directions: Combine tea bags and hot water in a 4-cup glass measuring cup, and steep 10 minutes.
Remove and discard tea bags.
Stir together tea, lemonade and limeade concentrates, mango nectar, and cold water.
Cover and chill 3 hours.
Serve over ice.

name: Wrong Island Iced Tea
directions: serve over ice in a high ball glass.

name: Peachy Lime Daiquiri
directions: Combine the peach, rum, peach liqueur, lime juice, and simple syrup in a blender with 2 cups of ice.
Blend until smooth.
Pour into to chilled cocktail glasses.
Garnish each with a lime wheel.

name: Kentucky Mimosa
directions: Stir together the sugar and 1/2 cup water in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Bring to a light boil, and cook, stirring occasionally, 3 minutes or until sugar is dissolved.
Remove from heat; add sage leaves, and let stand 5 minutes.
Remove and discard leaves.
Stir together the syrup, club soda, orange juice, and bourbon in a large pitcher; add ice cubes to fill or just serve in glasses over ice.
Serve immediately.

name: Thin Mint
directions: Mix the creme de menthe, creme de cacao, irish cream, vodka, and half-and-half in a pitcher and freeze for 1 hour.
Chill 6 martini glasses in the freezer for 30 minutes.
For each drink, shake a little more than 5 ounces of the mixture in a shaker with ice and strain into the chilled glasses or serve over ice in short glasses.
Garnish with chocolate shavings, if desired.
Make-ahead: this can be made up to 8 hours ahead and kept in the refrigerator.

name: Paloma
directions: Shake tequila and grapefruit juice in cocktail shaker.
Strain into tall glass.
Float Club Soda on top (do not stir into drink).
Enjoy!

name: Pomegranate-Ginger Margarita
directions: Combine the tequila, triple sec, pomegranate juice, lime juice, simple syrup, and ginger juice in a blender with 1 cup ice.
Blend until smooth; pour into a chilled cocktail or Margarita glass.
Garnish with candied ginger.

name: Hot Tiramisu Drink
directions: In a medium saucepan, whisk together the mascarpone cheese and the heavy cream. Add the milk and heat on low to medium heat, stirring frequently until heated through and completely smooth.
Add the unsweetened cocoa powder to the mixture and stir again for 5 minutes, or until the cocoa completely dissolves.
Pour hot coffee into each serving cup - the coffee should fill only the bottom quarter of the cup.
Pour the chocolate/cheese mixture over the top of the coffee. Add the maple syrup and stir until completely mixed.
Garnish with whipping cream, a splash of maple syrup, a sprinkle of coffee, a dusting of cocoa powder and a few chocolate chips.
Serve immediately, for authenticity, serve with a lady finger on the side.

name: Blue Bazooka Punch
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender, shake & serve.

name: Alabama Sunset
directions: With a wooden pestle or spoon, crush the blackberries with the sugar in a cocktail shaker.
Let macerate for a few minutes.
Add the brandy and strain into two champagne flutes.
Top each with 4 ounces of champagne.

name: Multiple Orgasm
directions: Fill glass half full of ice.
Add all the rest of the ingredients filling the glass with milk.
Add more alcohol for desired strength. I like mine strong!
ENJOY!
Tip 3 tablespoons is equal to 1 shot.

name: Frozen Holiday Bellinis
directions: Place frozen juice concentrate (undiluted) and frozen fruit in blender.
Pour in half of the Moscato and add enough ice to fill the blender.
Blend and Puree mixture until smooth. (if using cherries, add 1 Tbsp juice from jar for a light pink blush.).
Pour mixture into a large pitcher, stir in remaining Moscato, if desired, or save it for the next batch.  Serve in festive holiday glasses and top each with a cherry, if desired.  Cheers!

name: Blue Ice Martini
directions: Shake with ice, strain into a martini glass.  Garnish with a lemon twist.

name: White Chocolate Heaven
directions: Chill two small martini glasses (or one large one!).
Pour all ingredients into an ice-filled cocktail shaker.
Shake and pour into 2 chilled cocktail glasses, top with a pinch of cocoa powder, and serve.

name: Spiced Raspberry Tea
directions: Combine all ingredients in a medium mixing bowl; stir well.  Using a whisk helps achieve this.
Pour into a 1-pint wide-mouth jar with a tight-fitting lid a portion at a time, packing down lightly between layers.  Seal jar.

To Serve:  Spoon 1 to 1-1/2 tablespons Spiced Raspberry Tea Mix into a cup or mug.  Add 6 ounces boiling water; stir until tea mix is dissolved.  Serve hot or over ice.  You can garnish this with raspberries, orange slice and/or a sprig of mint, if desired.

name: Cherry Slurpee
directions: Pour 1 cup of the club soda into a blender.
Add the sugar, Kool-Aid mix and cherry extract.
Blend until all of the sugar is dissolved.
Add the crushed ice and blend on high until slushy and smooth.
Add the remaining club soda and blend briefly until mixed.
If necessary, put the blender into your freezer for 1/2 hour. This will help thicken it.
After 1/2 hour remove blender from freezer and, again, blend briefly to mix.

name: Grapefruit Cosmo
directions: In a shaker with ice, mix together the vodka, triple sec, grapefruit juice, and lime juice. Strain into a martini glass.
Upgrade: Add 1 teaspoon Campari to the shaker before straining.
Real Simple, DECEMBER 2007.

name: Keurig Hot Mocha Java
directions: Place chocolate pieces and sugar into mug.  Brew your favorite K-Cup into mg.  Let stand for 30 seconds, stirring occasionally until chocolate and sugar have melted.  You may add more sugar to taste.
Heat half and half or light cream until steaming, stirring occasionally.  Pour mixture into mug.  Add whipped cream o top, sprinkle with dash of cinnamon and add a cinnamon stick.

name: Keurig Brewed Iced Caffe Latte
directions: swirl chocolate syrup inside walls of clear 16 oz. cup.
Squirt chocolate syrup to the bottom of the cup.
Add a packet of sugar.
Pack cup with ice.
add about 2 oz half and half.
Brew your favorite k-Cup to half fill the cup.  You may also use strong coffee.
add whipped cream and drizzle chocolate syrup on the top.
you may add sprinkles!

name: Tranquilizing Tea (For Insomniacs and Others)
directions: Mix.
Keep in a tightly closed jar (in freezer).
Use 1 heaping teaspoon in 1 cup of boiling water.
Let it steep for 1 minute, strain.
Sweeten with honey, if desired.
Sip.

name: Mango Punch
directions: Combine all but lemon slices.
Serve right away over ice if you want and garnish with lemon slices.

name: Mango Orange Cooler
directions: Blend all.
Serve over crushed ice.

name: Garden Cooler
directions: Blend.
Drink.

name: Strawberry Shake
directions: Blend.
Drink.

name: Carrot Brew
directions: Blend.
Drink.

name: Lemon Grass Drink
directions: Trim the lemon grass and chop roughly.
Place water and lemon grass in a large saucepan.  Bring to a boil.  Remove from heat and stir in the sugar.  Cool to room temperature.
Strain.  Sugar to taste.
Chill or serve over ice.

name: Fiesta Cider
directions: Blend well.
Drink.

name: Bubble Tea
directions: Pour water over tapioca and let sit overnight to soften.  It will be gummy in texture.  Drain water.
In small saucepan combine the sugars and water over a medium lw heat.  Heat until sugar is dissolved.  Cool to room temperature.
Pour water over tea and steep 5 minutes.  Stain and cool to room temperature.
For each serving, put 1/4 cup each of tapioca, sugar syrup and half n half into a tall glass.  Add 8 oz of tea and stir to combine.  Add ice if you like it cold.
Traditionally it is served with a large straw so the tapioca can be enjoyed while sipping.

similar.name: Perfect Sweet Tea (Secret of the South)

name: Ninfa's Rita Drink
directions: This recipe is proportional.  Scale it to how much you want.
Mix and freeze in an ice cream freezer.  If you don't have one, just put it in the freezer and then scrape it into your glass.

name: Yogurt Delight
directions: Blend.
Drink.

similar.name: Yogurt Lassi With  Sweet or Savory Variations

name: Banana Nog
directions: Blend.
Drink.

name: Hot Spiced Tea in a Jar - Instant
directions: Mix well.
Fill 8 oz canning jars.
Label:
To make one serving: Add 1 heaping teaspoon to 1 cup hot water.

name: Spiced Christmas Tea (For Steeping)
directions: Mix.
Place in 8 oz jars.
Label.
A warm and spicy tea, it is best drunk without milk, although adding honey can enhance the taste even more.  Steep as you would any fine tea.

similar.name: Hot Spiced Tea in a Jar - Instant

name: Voter's Float
directions: In each of eight chilled glasses, place 1/2 cup of vanilla ice cream.
Add 1 cup of apple cider to each and sprinkle with nutmeg.
Serve immediately.

name: Moroccan Mint Tea
directions: Boil water.
Put mint and sugar into 8 oz mug and muddle or use a spoon to crush.
Add tea.
Pour water over it and stir.
Steep 5 minutes.
Stain.
Enjoy.

name: Sassy Water
directions: Blend.
Let sit overnight.

name: Digestion Assist
directions: Mix.

name: Holiday Isle Rum Runner
directions: Blend with ice.
Keep in freezer.
Serve in 12 oz glasses.

name: Italian Balloon Wine
directions: Pour 1/2 of the water from the jug and reserve.
Make a sludge out of the thawed grape juice and sugar.
Pour sludge into the jug with 1/2 gallon of water still in it.
Dissolve the yeast in some of the saved water, warmed.  Add to jug.
Fill jug to the top with reserved water.
Put the balloon over the mouth and seal it well with tape.
Within 2 hours the balloon will be full the the wine "boiling".
Ferment for 6-8 weeks.
Decant.
Wait 2 days.
Drink.

name: Cucumber and Ginger Cooler
directions: Muddle cucumber with lime juice in a cocktail shaker.
Add gin, ginger liqueur and agave syrup.
Fill 2/3 full with cubed ice.
Shake well.
Strain contents to a tall glass.
Top with club soda and stir gently.
Add ice as needed.
Garnish and serve.

name: Pomegranate Margarita
directions: Place all ingredients in cocktail shaker.
Fill 2/3 full with ice cubes.
Shake well.
Strain over fresh ice into 12 oz highball glass.
Garnish and serve.

name: Papaya Tea
directions: Simmer leaves in water until reduced by half.
Strain.
Enjoy or store in fridge.  It only keeps 3 days.
OR.
Use standard dehydrating methods to dry the leaves if you don't have it in your yard.

name: Banana Di Amore
directions: Blend with 1 cup crushed ice until frozen.
Serve in a nice stemmed glass.
Garnish with banana and orange slice.

name: Madagascar Ranonapango
directions: Malagache cooks double the quantity of rice they require for the meal. When it is cooked, they remove most of the rice from the earthenware pan. The remainder (a layer about 1/2-inch thick) is heated until it is burned and acquires a characteristic aroma. At that point boiling water is poured over the rice to the top of the pan. It is cooled, strained, and chilled. This beverage is used in place of water on the island.

name: Avo-Tini
directions: To make avocado pearls, use a small melon scoop.
In a cocktail shaker, combine all liquid and shake vigorously.  Strain into martini glass.
Garnish with avocado pearls and jicama wedge.

name: Banana Lover
directions: Blend until smooth.
Enjoy!

name: Banana Frost
directions: Blend until smooth.

name: Banana Bird
directions: Shake with ice and strain into a cocktail glass.

name: Morphing Martini (Color Changing)
directions: Yes. Red cabbage contains a water-soluble anthocyanin that is a pH indicator. At low pH (acid), it's red. It's purple at neutral pH, and goes blue then green as the solution becomes alkaline. You're not going to see the full range here, because we want the drink to be, well, drinkable.
To get the indicator, chop up a cabbage leaf, put it in a bowl with some water, and microwave until it's boiling (or just add boiling water and allow to steep). A purple pigment will stain the water.
Build:
Add a teaspoon of cabbage juice --  I mean indicator solution - to the martini glass. Then add very small quantities of baking soda, just enough to turn the solution blue.
Add gin (or vodka) and vermouth (~6:1) to the glass. You should have a pale blue clear liquid.
Present:

Give the recipient the drink. Tell them to watch closely, then add a chunk of dry ice. It will sink to the bottom of the drink, and bubble away happily, slowly cooling the drink. It will also neutralise the baking soda and change the colour of the martini.
Taste:

In the interests of science, I tried some of the baking soda/indicator mix straight. Baking soda tastes salty on its own, and rather overpowers the cabbage juice. The latter by itself doesn't taste of much; a slightly sweet, vegetably taste, nothing like what its violent colour suggests. Overall, it flavours the martini in a way reminiscent of a dirty martini (martini with olive brine added). So you'll only like it if you like dirty martinis, probably a pretty small constituency.

name: Creamed Papaya
directions: Remove skin and seeds from papaya and blend together with ice cream until smooth and creamy. Pour into stemmed glass and top with Creme de Cassis.

name: Turkish Coffee Brewing
directions: When brewing Turkish coffee, the foaming occurs at around 70C/158F, much cooler than boiling, which is why it's possible to foam the coffee repeatedly without boiling it--higher than 75C/167F the coffee becomes over-extracted.
Much has been written about the number of times the coffee foams: once, thrice, etc. This is very confusing: just as in other brewing, the time spent at brewing temperature is your best guide.

Instead of foaming and cooling cycles, modulate the flame to maintain a continuous foam for the duration of the extraction time. To me, it seems simpler and more consistent to use extraction time, as in other brewing styles.

Procedure:  Room temperature water with sugar, coffee, and cardamom stirred in is put onto the gas at medium heat. At two minutes, when foaming starts at the edges of the ibrik, slowly begin reducing the heat. The goal is to keep the coffee foaming, but not to let it rise more than a quarter of its volume. If you turn the gas down too quickly and the foaming stops, just turn it back up. The goal is to foam for 3 additional minutes (5 minutes total time). At 6 minutes total the coffee tastes overextracted, and at 4 it can be thin. The temperature at the end of 5 minutes should be around 75C/167°F.
At the end of extraction time, add just a touch of room temperature water to end the brewing--10% should be plenty.
Swirl the ibrik gently to help the grounds caught in the foam subside and place the ibrik in a saucer of water to cool.
After 1-2 minutes of settling, pour the coffee gently to retain the grounds. With a good brew, you should have enough foam to cover most or all of the surface of a demitasse cup.

Coffee amount:  Between 7-10% of initial water mass. At 10% the body is heavy and many will find it harsh. 8% is very nice. This method of brewing can accentuate the acidity of the beans--the coffee is very different from french press in flavor profile (this is not surprising since the extraction temperature is so much lower).
Sugar amount:  0-4% of water mass. Using half the mass of coffee is just about the maximum to balance the bitterness and really let the acidity shine.
Grinding:  Much better if ground with a burr grinder, powder fine.  Or try the Zass turkish mill, a manual one.

name: Coffee Foam in Microwave
directions: Find a jar with a lid and fill it with as much milk as you normally like in your coffee (or other hot beverage!). The milk should fill the jar no more than half way to allow room for the foam.
Shake the jar with the lid on as hard as you can until the milk has become frothy and doubled in size. This takes us about 30 seconds.
Take the lid off and microwave for 30 seconds. The foam will rise to the top of the milk and the heat from the microwave will help stabilize it.
Pour the milk into your coffee using a spoon to hold back the foam. Then scoop the foam on top!

name: Bloody Mary Bloody Biker
directions: Mix all the ingredients together and mix well.

name: Frantic Nuts
directions: 1. In a blender, combine vanilla ice cream, hazlenuts, milk and hazlenut liqueur. Blend until smooth. Pour into glasses and garnish with a dollop of whipped cream.

name: Key West Margarita
directions: Mix all in blender. Blend at slow speed until smooth.

name: Cucumber Mint and Basil Soda
directions: Bring water and sugar to a boil  to make simple syrup.
Keep 1.5 cups in a small saucepan and remove pan from heat.
Add cucumber slices (reserving 12 for garnish), 24 mint leaves, and basil.
Cover and let steep for 30 minutes.
Strain cucumber-herb syrup into a jar, pressing on solids in strainer.
Fill a 12-oz. glass with ice cubes. Add 2 tablespoons syrup and top with soda water (about 1/2 cup). Stir to combine. Garnish with cucumber slice and sprig of mint. Repeat to make 11 more sodas.

name: Lemongrass and Lime Leaf Soda
directions: Combine citrus zest, lime leaves, lemongrass, and 2 cups water in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain lemongrass mixture into a jar and chill. Fill a 12-oz. glass with ice cubes.
Add 2 tablespoons lemongrass mixture and 1 tablespoons simple syrup.
Top with soda water (about 1/2 cup) and stir to combine.

name: Strawberry Lemon and Basil Soda
directions: Combine fresh lemon juice, raw sugar, basil, whole strawberries, and salt in a pint glass. Muddle ingredients with a muddler or the handle of a wooden spoon until sugar dissolves. Add ice cubes and soda water and stir until chilled. Strain into a 12-oz. glass filled with ice cubes and garnish with a strawberry slice.

name: Spicy Ginger Soda
directions: Combine sugar, red pepper flakes, salt, ginger, and 2 cups water in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove pan from heat, cover, and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain ginger syrup into a jar and chill.
Fill a 12-oz. glass with ice cubes.
Add 2 tablespoons ginger syrup, squeeze a lime wedge into glass, and top with soda water.
Stir to combine.

name: Grandma's Whiskey Sour
directions: In a low cocktail glass (or if using high ball just double the recipe but it'll be strong!).
add ice cubes first (handful).
add the first four ingredients and stir well.
top with a cherry and twist and enjoy!

similar.name: Whiskey, Passionfruit and Lemon Iced Tea

name: Cactus Creek Koyote Kiwi Margarita
directions: Start with a shaker of ice. Add all other ingredients then either blend or shake and stir - serving on the rocks. Be sure to salt the rim of the glass and garnish with a kiwi slice. Enjoy.

name: A Wee Bit of Summer Cocktail
directions: Fill a 24 oz glass with ice. Add the liquor, then equal parts pineapple juice and lemon lime soda. Garnish with a lemon wheel and cherry.

similar.name: Jamaican Coconut Water & White Rum Cocktail

name: Watermelon Splash
directions: In a chilled glass, combine soda, voda, and a splash or two of cranberry juice.
Add ice; stir to combine.

name: Mango Madras
directions: In a glass, combine the cranberry juice, orange juice, and rum; add ice.
Garnish with skewered orange wedge and cherries.

name: Muy Fuerte Margarita
directions: Shake ingredients in a cocktail shaker, serve in a salt-rimmed glass over ice.

name: Vista Blue
directions: Mix and serve in a chilled glass.

name: Pomegranate Martini
directions: Shake all liquid ingredients together and garnish with a lime wedge.

name: Cherry Margarita
directions: Fill a 16 oz glass 3/4-way full with ice.
Pour in tequila.
Fill the glass with equal parts cherry juice and margarita mix.
Serve on the rocks or blended.

name: Ginger Ale Ginger Soda Ginger Pop
directions: Chop up the ginger root, use 3 oz if you want it stronger.
Simmer the ginger, juices, and sugars in 1 ½  quart of water for 30 mintues to an hour. The longer you leave it simmer, the stronger it will be.
Remove heat and strain through a kitchen strainer.
Mix the brew with the other 3 quarts of water.
Let it all cool till it’s lukewarm.
Inside a small jar, stir in 1/8 teaspoon of yeast in with 1⁄4 cup warm water (not hot!) Bread yeast will work but ale yeast from a homebrew store is preferred.
After 15 minutes, add the yeast solution to your lukewarm brew.
Sit for 10 minutes.
Bottle it in 8 - 12 oz bottles.
Bottling:
Make sure you CLEAN your bottles inside and out, rinse and air dry.
How to use plastic bottles:
Pour in the brew and twist the caps on tight.
Squeeze the bottles and notice how they give.
Every few hours, squeeze them and see how they are giving. They will get harder to squeeze. When they no longer give any more, put them in the fridge.
Drink cold and enjoy!
If you used glass bottles, put them in the fridge after a day.
Don't want to use yeast?  Substitute a carbonated water like club or seltzer water in place of that extra 3 quarts of water.

name: Snickertini
directions: Drizzle a thin line of caramel sauce and chocolate sauce on the inside of a  old-fashioned glass (or short "rocks" tumbler).  Place in refrigerator to chill.
Combine caramel vodka, Irish cream, chocolate liqueur, amaretto, cream and ice cubes in a cocktail shaker; shake well.
Pour (unstrained) into prepared glass.  Serve with a straw.

name: Kahlua
directions: In a medium saucepan heat the 2 1/2 cups water, brown sugar and sugar.  Stir until dissolved.
In a bowl mix the instant coffee, 1 cup warm water, vanilla and chocolate extract.
Add instant coffee mix to the saucepan and cool.
Add vodka and bottle (dark bottles are needed), putting 1 vanilla bean in each bottle.
Keep bottled approximately 4-5 weeks before opening, and enjoy!

name: Caramel Macchiato
directions: Combine milk, syrup and caramel then heat. DO NOT BOIL.Heat in pot whisking till frothy or put in mug and microwave about 90seconds.
If heating in pot pour into mug. There should be foam on the top.If microwaving in mug use a milk frother to create foam on top of the milk.
Pour prepared espresso into milk.
Top with caramel drizzle.

name: Black Tea With Lemon Balm
directions: In a saucepan, bring water to a boil. Remove from the heat. Add tea, lemon balm and lemon peel. Let stand 5 minutes. Remove tea bag and strain tea into a teacup or mug.  Sweeten to taste.

name: Jp-8
directions: In a cocktail shaker, fill half with ice and then each liqour.  Shake and strain into a rocks glass or a 250 ml glass beaker.  Shooter is the same color as JP-8.  Drink Responsibly.

name: Pina' Colada Punch
directions: Combine first 5 ingredients in a 5-quart ice cream bucket.
Freeze for 3 days.
Stir every day.
When ready, fill a glass half way with slush.
Fill the rest of the way with 7-Up.

name: Samoan - Orange Passion Fruit - Ade
directions: Mix the fruit juices together and add sugar syrup to taste.
The amount of syrup you will use will depend on you as the passionfruit is strong and tart in flavor.  Tropical fruits are generally tarter.
Next add the cold soda water and serve in large glasses filled with ice.
Garnish with an orange slice and enjoy.
This is best when served cold.

name: Lychee Lady Cocktail – a Tropical Martini from the Island
directions: Pour the ingredients into a cocktail shaker, add ice and shake.
Then pour the liquid into a martini glass and add a dash of grenadine syrup.
Garnish with a tropical blossom and serve.

name: Coffee-Cocoa Infused Vodka
directions: 1.In a jar with a tight-fitting lid, combine the vodka with the espresso beans and cocoa nibs. Close the jar tightly and let stand at room temperature for 2 weeks. Strain the vodka through a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids. Strain the vodka a second time through a coffee filter into a clean jar and refrigerate for up to 3 months.

name: Raspberry Infused Vodka
directions: In a jar with a tight-fitting lid, combine the vodka with the raspberries. Close the jar tightly and let stand at room temperature for 2 weeks. Strain the vodka through a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids. Strain the vodka a second time through a coffee filter into a clean jar and refrigerate for up to 3 months.

name: Cappuccino Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Store in airtight container or into pretty jars for gifts.
To prepare one serving: Add 3 tbs mix to 6 oz hot water.
Stir well and enjoy.

name: Iced Coffee
directions: In a large bowl, dissolve coffee in water. Add sweetener if desired. Stir in the milk, chocolate syrup and vanilla; mix well. Serve over ice.

similar.name: Make Iced Coffee in Five Minutes

name: Amarula Coffee Surprise
directions: Pour the coffee in large coffee mugs. Pour the Amarula Cream in each coffee, followed by the whipped cream, brown sugar and marshmallows. Sprinkle with hot chocolate powder and extra Amarula Cream. Top with grated chocolate if desired.
Conversions: 1 tot = 30ml = 1 oz = 3cl = 1 shot.

name: Spiced Coconut Coffee
directions: Combine the ground coffee, red pepper, cloves, and cinnamon stick in a coffee filter set into a drip coffee brewer.
Pour the water into the brewer’s water reservoir.
Set the coffee brewer on to brew.
While the coffee brews, gently warm the coconut milk in a small saucepan over medium-low heat.
Stir in the honey until the honey is dissolved.
Pour the brewed coffee into the mixture; stir. Divide the liquid into two mugs to serve.

name: Pacific Sunset
directions: Combine juices in a blender and pour over ice in a highball glass.
Add grenadine.
Garnish with lime wedge if desired.

name: Acapulco
directions: Add tequila, rum, grapefruit juice, and pineapple juice to a shaker filled with ice.
Shake vigorously.
Strain into an ice-filled highball glass.
Garnish with nasturtium blossom, if desired.

name: Wiki Waki Woo
directions: Combine ingredients with ice, pour over ice in a tall glass. Garnish with cherry or pineapple slice. and hang on to your hat.

name: British Bulldog
directions: Combine in a chilled, 16 oz glass.
Add alcohol.
Fill with cola.
Top with a splash of cream.
Garnish with a maraschino cherry.

name: Moscow Mule
directions: Fill a Boston shaker 1/2 way with ice. Add vodka, lime juice and bitters. Fit with the other half of the shaker, and shake until condensation forms on the outside. Strain into a glass (or copper mug).
Add ginger beer and squeeze in lime wedge. Stir and serve.

name: Ginger Beer Shandy (Alcoholic)
directions: Freeze eight 16-oz beer glasses or beer mugs for at least 2 hours.
Rub rims of glasses or mugs with lemon slices and dip rims into finely chopped candied ginger.
In a large pitcher combine 3 Tbsp candied ginger and lemon juice.
Muddle together with a wooden spoon or muddler.
Pour in beer and ginger ale.
Stir gently to combine.
Serve in frosted glasses or mugs.
Garnish with additional lemon slices, if desired.

name: Razzy Lager
directions: Freeze eight 16 oz beer glasses for at least 2 hours.
In a large pitcher combine 2 cups raspberries and the Chambord.
Muddle with a wooden spoon or muddler.
Pour beer over raspberry mixture.
Stir gently to combine.
Serve in frosted glasses.
If desired, thread additional raspberries onto small picks or skewers and place into drinks as a garnish.

name: Cassis Martini
directions: Combine vodka, cassis, pink grapefruit juice, and ice in a shaker. Shake vigorously, and strain into a martini glass.

name: Muddled Cherry Syrup
directions: Using a muddler or potato masher, smash cherries and sugar in a medium bowl to release juices (it's okay to leave some cherries whole). Stir in 3/4 cup hot water. Let stand until slightly cooled, stirring occasionally, about 30 minutes. Cover and chill. DO AHEAD Can be made 1 week ahead. Keep chilled.
Strain syrup before using, reserving cherries for garnish.

similar.name: Cherry or Mixed Berry Syrup for SodaStream

name: Bourbon Cherry Old Fashioned
directions: Using a muddler or the handle of a wooden spoon, smash cherries and orange peel in a cocktail shaker until cherries are well mashed.
Add bourbon, cherry syrup, and orange bitters. Fill with ice and shake vigorously 10 times. Strain into a Martini glass. Garnish with a cherry from the syrup and an orange twist.

name: Grass Skirt Cocktail
directions: Pour ingredients in a cocktail shaker filled half way with ice, shake from the ships, Hula style, pour through strainer into a cocktail or old-fashioned glass, garnish with a cherry. Enjoy!

name: Lemon Deliciousness
directions: Fill a collins-size glass with ice cubes. Pour in the vodka and Limoncello. Fill glass with ginger ale. Stir and enjoy!

name: Poolside Passion
directions: Muddle lemon wedges, strawberries, and simple syrup in a shaker.
Add vodka, splash of cranberry juice, and ice, and shake 20 times.
Strain into a chilled martini glass, and garnish with flower.

name: Balsamic Martini from Tahiti
directions: In a blender (or mixer), half fill with ice.
Add the vodka, Martini dry, strawberry purée and the balsamic vinegar.
Mix all the ingredients into a frozen cocktail (20 seconds).
Pour into a Margarita glass.
Decorate at your choice.

name: Mahana Cocktail from Tahiti
directions: Blend all ingredients.
Serve in a Burgundy glass with a pineapple wedge garnish.

name: Chef Todd's Bloody Mary
directions: Shake hard with a glass full of ice and serve in a pint glass rimmed with kosher salt and garnished with a stuffed olive, pepperocini, pickled onion and a kosher pickle spear.
Note: Veal Demi Glace or a mixture of a beef bouillon cube dissolved in water can be substituted for the beef stock.
Also, the horseradish specified in the recipe was Beaver Atomic Horseradish. I was unable to include the name in the list of ingredients.

name: Swiss Alps Cocktail
directions: Shake ingredients together with ice, strain and pour into a chilled glass.

name: Kato Tea
directions: Pour the tea into a hurricane glass over ice.
Add the Malibu mango rum, stir and serve.

name: Hana Passion
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender cup with ice and blend until smooth.

name: Mint Chocolate Coffee
directions: Brew your regular coffee. Add the 1 ounce of chocolate mint liqueur into your cup. Add some whipping cream if desired. Sprinkle with shaved chocolate.

name: Pepper-Vodka Bloody Mary
directions: Half fill a cocktail shaker with small ice cubes.
Add in tomato juice, vodka, horseradish, Worcestershire sauce, Tabasco, celery salt, and pepper.
Put the top on and shake.
Pour out into a large glass.
Squeeze in the lemon wedge, garnish with the pickled asparagus spear; serve.

name: Orange Spice Tea Mix
directions: Stir all ingredients together.  Store in an airtight container.
To use:  Add about 2 heaping tablespoons to a mug or cup of hot water.  Stir thoroughly.

name: Tropical Cooler
directions: In a pitcher blend first 6 ingredients.
When ready to serve add ginger ale.

name: The Whitney Babe
directions: Combine all your ingredients, and brew your pot of coffee by your favorite method.
Using a French press is traditional for this coffee recipe though not required - yea, enjoy!

name: Misae's Japanese Dance
directions: Heat ingredients together until honey and sugar are dissolved. On a low simmer heat for another 2 minutes.
Strain out the seeds and serve into demitasse cups. A wonderful oriental blend you'll always remember.
Serves 4.

name: Coffee Soda Train
directions: Mix coffee, sugar and half & half.
Fill 4 tall glasses about halfway.
Add 1 scoop of ice cream to each, then fill up with soda.
Top with whipped cream. Serves 4.

name: Toblerone Cocktail
directions: Blend and serve over ice or blend with ice for a frosted drink.  Drizzle top with chocolate syrup.

name: Put the Lime in the Coconut Cocktail
directions: Add all ingredients, except coconut flakes and honey, in a cocktail shaker with ice.
Pour honey onto a plate.
Lay out coconut on a plate.
Dip the rims of chilled glasses into honey and roll in coconut.
Shake and pour into chilled martini glasses to serve.
NOTE: For a non-alcoholic version, omit the vodka and the banana Schnapps and substitute the ice with 1 banana. Puree the mixture in a blender.

similar.name: Put the Lime in the Coconut

name: Mint Basil Cucumber Simple Syrup
directions: Combine water and sugar in sauce pan.
Bring to simmer and stir until sugar has dissolved.
Turn heat off and add cucumber, basil and mint.
Remove from heat, place lid on pan and leave overnight, (longer the mixture sits, the better it tastes).
Strain and store in fridge.
Add 2 tbsp of syrup to 8 oz. of good cold water.

name: Hot Buttered Bouquet, Coffee
directions: Put butter in an Irish coffee glass or mug, then pour in the steaming coffee. Add brown sugar and stir until dissolved.
Add clove and rum.
Dust with nutmeg.

similar.name: Spiced Coffee With Brandied Whipped Cream

name: Mango Mania
directions: Chop the mango, pour into a blender with the rest of the ingredients.
Blend until smooth, and serve in a tall glass.
Garnish with a sprinkling of ground black pepper.

name: Bali Hai Cocktail
directions: Put all ingredients except the champagne in a cocktail shaker with cracked ice and shake well. Pour into zombie style glasses and top off with champagne.

name: Blue Banner Wave Cocktail
directions: Put ice cubes in shaker.
Add white cranberry juice, blue curacao, and silver tequila.
Shake and strain into glass.
Garnish with edible flower.

name: Fireworks Flair Cocktail
directions: BLUEBERRY ICE CUBES:
In ice cube mold, fill 4 molds half-way with water. Freeze for 2 hours.  Drop in 3 blueberries. Add water to fill those cubes.  Place in freezer for 48 hours to set.
COCKTAIL:
Place 4 plain ice cubes in cocktail shaker.  Add cranberry juice, vodka, blackberry brandy, and triple sec.  Shake; strain into glass.
Add blueberry ice cubes.
Garnish with strip of lemon rind in an S-pattern around blueberry then raspberry.

name: Sweet Lime Iced Tea
directions: Pour the water into a gallon sized jar over the tea bags.
Allow to steep for 45 minutes.
Remove and discard the tea bags.
Stir in the sugar and lime juice until the sugar has dissolved.
Cool to room temperature; refrigerate until cold before serving.

name: Raspberry Iced Tea
directions: Bring the water to a boil in a large pot.
Stir in the tea bags, raspberries, and sugar until the sugar has dissolved.
Allow the mixture to steep until desired level of tea flavor is reached, 3-5 minutes and remove tea bags.
Stir in lemonade mix until dissolved.
Pour tea into pitchers and add ice to cool.

name: Yummy Iced Coffee
directions: Put all ingredients into a blender and blend until smooth.

name: Dublin Dream
directions: In an Irish coffee glass, place all ingredients except the whipped cream.  Stir well until mixed and garnish with whipped cream.

name: Caribou Lou
directions: Shake all ingredients and serve in a highball glass. Best served if pineapple juice is cold.
Serve in a highball glass.

name: Balding Pussy
directions: Pour all ingredients in shaker and shake.
Strain into glass and serve.

name: Blue Heaven
directions: Mix liquid ingredients with ice filled cocktail shaker. Shake until chilled.
Strain into chilled martini glass with sugar on the rim.
Garnish with both a fresh slice of orange and pineapple.
Serve In: A martini Glass.

name: Kamekaazi
directions: Pour ingredients into a ice filled shaker and shake it until the shaker is very cold.
Strain the drink into a shot glass.

name: Singapore Sling
directions: Fill a glass to the rim with ice cubes.
Pour cherry brandy, grenadine, plymouth gin, soda water and sour mix into a shaker.
Fill the shaker with ice cubes and shake it until the shaker is very cold.
Strain into glass.
Garnish with a cocktail umbrella , a whole maraschino berry, a slice pineapple, a pineapple leaf, a pineapple spear, and one straw.

name: Fat Box
directions: Pour all ingredients into a ice filled shaker.
Shake until chilled then strain into a shot glass.

name: Blueberry Lemonade
directions: Pour the vodka and blueberry schnapps into a cocktail shaker half-filled with ice cubes. Add sweet and sour mix and cranberry juice.
Sweeten to taste with sugar.
Shake well.
Strain into a chilled cocktail glass.
Garnish with a lemon twist, and serve.

name: Chicago Fizz
directions: Shake all ingredients (except carbonated water) with ice and strain into a highball glass over two ice cubes.
Fill with carbonated water, stir, and serve.

name: Chocolate Cherry Bomb
directions: Layer this drink: grenadine, then the Cacao, then the cream.
Shoot.
Tastes just like a chocolate covered cherry.

name: Chumbawumba
directions: Pour a basic snake bite of half cider and lager in a pint glass.
Then add to the top a shot of whiskey and vodka.
Stir and then drink!
Serve in a Beer Mug.

name: Farmer Giles
directions: In a mixing glass half-filled with ice cubes, combine the gin, dry vermouth, sweet vermouth, and bitters.
Stir well. Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with the lemon twist.

name: Frosty Pineapple Crush
directions: Pour the Absolut Citron vodka, pineapple vodka and pineapple juice into a cocktail shaker half-filled with ice cubes.
Shake and strain into a collins glass 1/4 filled with crushed ice. Pour the RedRum rum on top.
Put a slice of pineapple and a cherry on an umbrella and place on the side of glass as garnish.

name: Watermelon Spritzer
directions: Place watermelon in a blender.  Cover and process until blended.  Strain and discard pulp; transfer juice to a pitcher.  Stir in limeade concentrate.  Refrigerate for 6 hours or overnight.
Just before serving, stir in carbonated water.  Garnish servings with lime slices.

name: Amaretto Cloud
directions: froth milk with battery operated milk frother.
spoon froth on top of amaretto to create the cloud effect.
Serve with an umbrella, swizel stick, or short straw so the drinker can play with it.
NOTE: There is always left over milk that didn't get bubbles in it, don't use or you'll muddy the amaretto before it gets to the drinker.
HINT:  Heat up the milk for a Amaretto Summer Cloud.
HINT: spin equal parts ice and milk in a blender or magic bullet for a Amaretto Snow Cloud.
HINT: Double or triple the amount of Amaretto for a stronger drink.

name: Mango-Cucumber Cooler
directions: Stir sugar and 1 1/2 tablespoons hot water in a large pitcher until dissolved.
Add wine, cucumber, and mango. Be sure the mango is firm.
Remove leaves from 6 sprigs of mint; stir into pitcher.
Cover and chill for at least 3 hours and up to 6 hours.  When the fruit sinks, the wine is ready.
Stir ginger ale into pitcher.
You can serve over ice or ladle it out so everyone gets mango and cucumbers in their glass.
Garnish with remaining 6 mint sprigs.

name: Summer Nights Punch
directions: Muddle the berries in the ginger syrup and orange juice. Add remaining ingredients and shake well with ice and strain over ice. Garnish: sliced strawberries and blackberries.

name: Ginger Lime Fizz With Blueberries
directions: Bring ginger, sugar, and 1 1/2 cups water to a boil in medium saucepan. Reduce heat to low and simmer 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and cool. Pour mixture through fine strainer, pushing down on solids to extract all syrup. Stir in lime juice and blueberries.
To serve: Pour 1/4 cup blueberry syrup into tall, ice-filled glass, and ad 2 Tbs vodka. Top with w/ Tbs club soda.

name: Minted Lemonade Punch
directions: Place sugar, mint, lemon zest, and 1 cup water in medium saucepan, and bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to low, and simmer 5 minutes, stirring until sugar dissolves. Cool, then strain. Stir in lemon juice.
Pour lemon syrup into large pitcher. Stir in strawberries, grapes, cucumber, and Pimm's liqueur.
To serve, divide mixture among 6 ice-filled glasses, and top each with 2 Tbs sparkling water.

name: Mint Syrup for Mint Juleps or Mojitos
directions: Combine water and sugar in a medium sauce pan and bring to a boil.
Once the liquid is boiling and the sugar has dissolved (creating simple syrup), remove from heat.
Rip the mint leaves into small pieces and combine with the hot simple syrup immediately after removing the pot from the heat.
Allow the mixture to cool until the pot is cool to the touch.
Now, it will be ready to add to a drink!
Refrigerate the left-overs.

name: Vanilla Bean Cream Soda
directions: Combine 1 cup of water with the sugar in a medium saucepan. Scrape the seeds from the vanilla bean and add . Boil over medium-high heat, until the sugar dissolves. Let syrup cool before stirring in the vanilla extract. In a large pitcher or bowl, combine with soda water, stirring gently to combine.
*Cherry-Cream Soda: Add 1 packet of cherry kool-aid and 1/2 teaspoons almond extract for a beautiful, bright red soda/.

name: Caramel Apple Spiced Cider
directions: Use a 2-quart slow cooker; pour the apple cider into the slow cooker and add the caramel syrup.
Add the spices; cover and cook on LOW for 4 hours or High for 2 hours, until heated through.
Stir well and ladle into mugs; top with a dollop of sweetened whipped cream.

name: Irish Coffee
directions: Use a 2-quart slow cooker; combine the coffee and Irish Cream in the slow cooker.
Stir in the whipping cream and cocoa powder (I like my coffee sweet, so I would taste and sweeten, if needed).
Cover and cook on LOW for 3 hours, or on HIGH for 90 minutes.
Serve in mugs with a dollop of sweetened whipped cream (the stuff in the can).

name: Luncheon Lemonade
directions: Use a 4-quart slow cooker; pour the water and juices into the slow cooker.
Stir in the sugar; add the whole cloves and the cinnamon stick.
Float the lemon slices on top.
Cover and cook on LOW for 3-4 hours, or until heated through.
To serve guests, provide a ladle, leave the lid off, and turn the slow cooker to LOW; your drink will not stay hot enough if left on the warm setting.

name: Butterscotch Mulled Cider
directions: In a 6-quart slow cooker, combine all the ingredients.
Cover and cook on LOW for 3-4 hours or until heated through.

name: Old Fashioned Cocktail
directions: Muddle the cherry and the orange slice in a bucket glass - then discard the fruit. Add the syrup and bitters. Drop in the desired amount of ice - I like lots for a super cold drink.
Pour the bourbon into the glass over the back of a spoon. Garnish with an orange flag and a lemon twist.

name: Citrus Julep
directions: Scrub the fruit and use a zester or sharp knife to scrape slivers of rind from each. Set the fruit aside.
Combine sugar and water in a saucepan and bring to a quick boil.
Add zest, turn heat to low and simmer for 2 minutes. Turn off heat, and allow to cool.
Put mint leaves in a quart jar and use the back of a wooden spoon to bruise them slightly, releasing the fragrance.
Pour syrup, including the zest, over the leaves, and steep for 1 hour.

Juice the lime, orange and lemon, and strain into a pitcher. Pour the mint syrup through the strainer as well, using the back of the spoon to press the captured mint leaves and zest to extract remaining juices.
For each person, fill an 8-ounce glass or julep cup with ice. Pour 3 ounces of bourbon into each, then top off with the mint syrup.
Serve with short straws and a garnish of mint.

name: White Chai Tea
directions: Put water and spices (except for the white tea) into a small pot.
Add water and cook on medium for 6 minutes, then add white tea.
Place lid on pot and cook for 6 minutes.
Add in milk and stir, then turn off the stove burner.
Add sugar and stir until completely dissolved.
Strain spices out and enjoy!

name: Almond Hot Toddy
directions: Over low heat, combine Vanilla Almond Breeze®, rum, brandy, almond extract, cinnamon and nutmeg in a medium saucepan, stirring constantly with a whisk until very warm.
Pour into large mugs, leaving an inch of room to the top.
Top with whipped cream, almonds and a sprinkle of nutmeg.

name: The Haymaker's Drink
directions: Mix all together in large pitcher and chill, or just put it in a jug and head out to the field!

name: Margarita Madras
directions: Combine the orange juice, tequila, triple sec, and cranberry juice in a cocktail shaker filled with 3/4 cup ice.
Shake vigorously.
Pour into a glass.
Garnish with an orange wedge.
Variation-for a pink-hued rim, dip the rim of the glass in grenadine before coating with sugar.

name: Island Margarita
directions: Combine the ingredients in a cocktail shaker filled with 3/4 cup ice.
Shake vigorously.
Pour into a cocktail glass.

name: Lake Austin Iced Tea
directions: Combine all ingredients in a 1 gallon container; blend well.
Serve in tall glasses over crushed ice.

name: Ruby Red Basil Cocktail
directions: Combine all ingredients in a cocktail shaker. Add ice, and shake.
Strain into a martini glass, top with a fresh basil leaf.

name: Mojito Refresher
directions: Place grapes and spearmint leave in a cocktail shaker or lidded cup. Mash/muddle the grapes and leaves.
Squeeze 1/2 lime into cup.
Add simple syrup and then pour in vodka and about 6 ice cubes.
Close shaker or lidded cup and shake vigorously at least 12 times.
Strain into glass and top with gingerale.
You can garnish with a mint leaf or lime slice if you're being creative (which I rarely am).

name: White Chocolate Martini - Pete Evans
directions: Place all the ingredients in a shaker over ice.
Shake and then strain into a martini glass and garnish with the marbled chocolate.

name: Marbled Chocolate Shard Garnish for Cocktails - Pete Evans
directions: Melt the dark chocolate and white chocolate in separate bowls.
Line 2 baking trays with baking paper.
Drizzle over the dark chocolate, then drizzle over the white chocolate.
Using a spatula, spread the chocolate to 1mm thick creating a marbled effect.
Cool slightly, then score into large squares.
Refrigerate to set.

similar.name: Morta De Chocolata Gelatini  (Death by Chocolate Martini)

name: Sugar Syrup for Light or Dark Spirits - Pete Evans
